# Agent Deals
> The definitive directory of 229+ deals, free tiers, and startup programs for AI agent developers.
> Last updated: 2026-03-15
> JSON API: /deals.json


## 🧠 LLM APIs & Model Providers
Large language model APIs that power agent reasoning, planning, and generation. Includes hosted inference, open-weight model serving, and model routing.

### Anthropic Claude API
URL: https://www.anthropic.com/api
Type: startup-credits
Deal: $5 in free API credits for new accounts; startup program via VC partners offers $25,000+ in credits; education program available for universities
Limits: $5 one-time credits on new accounts (credit card required for continued use). Startup program: $25,000 in credits for VC-backed startups. Not a recurring free tier.
Agent Notes: Claude excels at long-context agentic tasks (200K token context), tool use, and multi-step reasoning. Claude Code and the extended thinking feature are critical for agent frameworks like LangChain and CrewAI.
Discount: $25,000 in credits via VC partner startup program; up to $150,000+ by stacking programs
Eligibility: Early-stage startups backed by Anthropic partner VCs; bootstrapped founders also eligible for some tiers
Apply: https://www.anthropic.com/startups

### Anthropic Claude Code
URL: https://www.anthropic.com/claude-code
Type: open-source
Deal: Free to use with your own Anthropic API key. Open-source CLI tool — pay only API costs.
Limits: Free, open-source CLI. Pay only for Claude API tokens at standard rates (Claude Sonnet ~$3/M input, $15/M output). BYOK from Anthropic, AWS Bedrock, or GCP Vertex. Direct terminal-based coding agent with full filesystem and git access.
Agent Notes: Anthropic's own agentic coding tool using Claude directly in the terminal. No subscription — pure token costs. Deeply integrated with Claude's extended context and tool use. Competitive with Cursor/Windsurf for complex refactoring tasks. Increasingly used as a standalone agentic coding session tool.
Eligibility: All users with Anthropic API key
Apply: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code

### Baseten
URL: https://www.baseten.co
Type: free-tier
Deal: Basic (Startup) plan is pay-as-you-go with no platform fee. New accounts receive free credits to experiment. Offers both Model APIs (per-token) and Dedicated Deployments (per-minute GPU billing).
Limits: New accounts: free starter credits (historically $30). Basic plan: no monthly fee; pay per token or per GPU-minute. T4: $0.01052/min; H100: $0.10833/min. Model APIs start at $0.10/M tokens (GPT OSS 120B).
Agent Notes: Optimized for production LLM inference with fast cold starts and autoscaling. Supports custom fine-tuned models plus pre-optimized popular models. Great for agents that need dedicated GPU endpoints with guaranteed latency SLAs. Truss framework simplifies model packaging.
Discount: Free credits for new workspaces; enterprise starts at ~$5K/mo on AWS Marketplace
Eligibility: New Baseten accounts

### Cerebras Inference API
URL: https://cerebras.ai/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with no credit card required. Access to Llama 3.3 70B, Qwen 3 32B, and other models on Cerebras Wafer-Scale Engine hardware.
Limits: Free tier: 30 RPM, ~1,000,000 tokens/day. Developer tier has 10x higher rate limits. Fastest publicly available inference (often cited as 20x faster than OpenAI/Anthropic).
Agent Notes: Cerebras WSE delivers the fastest inference available, critical for real-time multi-agent systems and streaming. 70B-parameter models run at speeds previously only possible with smaller models, enabling more capable open-source agent pipelines.
Discount: Free tier, no credit card required
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://cloud.cerebras.ai/

### Cohere API
URL: https://cohere.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free Trial API key automatically created on signup. Rate-limited free access for development and experimentation. Startup program offers up to $10,000 in credits.
Limits: Trial key is rate-limited (exact rates not published). Not for production or commercial use on trial key. Production API key requires paid billing.
Agent Notes: Cohere specializes in enterprise RAG and semantic search. Command R+ excels at RAG with citations. Embed v3 is optimized for retrieval tasks in agent memory systems. Strong reranking models for improving RAG precision.
Discount: $10,000 in credits via startup program
Eligibility: Early-stage startups; apply via startup program
Apply: https://cohere.com/startups

### Cohere Embed
URL: https://cohere.com/embed
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free trial API key on account creation, rate-limited for development. Part of Cohere's general API trial.
Limits: Trial key rate-limited (exact limits not published). Not for production/commercial use without paid key. Embed v3 models support int8/binary quantization for storage efficiency.
Agent Notes: Cohere Embed v3 supports int8 and binary quantization, reducing storage and search costs by up to 4x without significant accuracy loss. Multilingual Embed v3 covers 100+ languages — critical for global agent deployments. Includes a Rerank API for improving RAG retrieval precision.
Discount: Free trial API key on signup
Eligibility: All new users
Apply: https://cohere.com/

### DeepSeek API
URL: https://platform.deepseek.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: 5 million free tokens for new users with no credit card required. Then pay-as-you-go at $0.028/M input tokens (cache hit) or $0.28/M (cache miss), $0.42/M output — up to 95% cheaper than GPT-4o.
Limits: 5M free tokens on signup (covers both deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner). Automatic context caching. 128K context window.
Agent Notes: DeepSeek V3.2 and R1 offer frontier-level reasoning at a fraction of the cost of GPT-4o or Claude Sonnet. Context caching is automatic and saves 90% on repeated prompts — crucial for long-running agent loops with stable system prompts. Also open-source for self-hosting.
Discount: 5M free tokens on signup, no credit card required
Eligibility: All new users

### Fireworks AI
URL: https://fireworks.ai/
Type: free-tier
Deal: $1 in free credits on signup for serverless inference. Access to open-source models including Llama 4, Mixtral, Qwen, and function-calling optimized models.
Limits: $1 one-time free credits on new account. Postpaid billing after credits exhausted. No recurring free tier.
Agent Notes: FireFunction-v2 is optimized for parallel function calling in agents. Fireworks supports compound AI systems, batching, and LoRA fine-tuning. Best for production agentic workloads requiring reliable function-call structured outputs.
Discount: $1 in free credits on signup
Eligibility: All new users

### Google A2A Protocol
URL: https://github.com/google-a2a/A2A
Type: open-source
Deal: Fully open-source protocol (Apache 2.0 license). Free to implement, no API costs. Donated to the Linux Foundation after launch by Google Cloud in April 2025.
Limits: Protocol spec is free. Implementation costs depend on your agent infrastructure. Built on HTTP, SSE, JSON-RPC—no proprietary dependencies.
Agent Notes: A2A (Agent2Agent) is the emerging industry standard for agent-to-agent communication. Enables agents from different vendors/frameworks to discover each other via 'Agent Cards' (JSON capability manifests), delegate tasks, share context, and coordinate across enterprise systems. Over 50 technology partners including LangChain, Salesforce, SAP, Workday. Complements MCP (Anthropic's tool protocol).
Discount: Free open-source protocol; no licensing fees
Eligibility: All developers; open-source with Apache 2.0 license

### Google Agent Development Kit (ADK)
URL: https://google.github.io/adk-docs/
Type: open-source
Deal: Free and open-source (Apache 2.0). Pay only for Gemini API calls (generous free tier on Google AI Studio).
Limits: ADK framework: free, open-source. Google AI Studio free tier: Gemini 2.0 Flash at 15 RPM free, 1,500 requests/day. Google Cloud Vertex AI: pay-per-token. Python and Java SDKs. Multi-agent orchestration, streaming, built-in evaluation. Launched 2025.
Agent Notes: Google's official agent framework launched in 2025. Native integration with Gemini models, Google Search grounding, and Google Cloud services. Multi-agent orchestration with parent-child agent hierarchies. MCP support. Significant free tier when combined with Google AI Studio's free Gemini quota.
Eligibility: All users — open source
Apply: https://github.com/google/adk-python

### Google Cloud for Startups
URL: https://cloud.google.com/startup
Type: startup-credits
Deal: Start tier: $2,000 in GCP credits for unfunded startups. Scale tier: up to $200,000 over 2 years for funded startups (pre-seed to Series A). AI tier: up to $350,000 over 2 years for AI-focused startups.
Limits: Start: $2K credits, 1 year, no funding required, <5 years old. Scale: Year 1 100% up to $100K + Year 2 20% up to $100K. AI Tier: Year 1 100% up to $250K + Year 2 20% up to $100K. Plus $12K Enhanced Support credits.
Agent Notes: GCP credits cover Vertex AI (agent hosting, model APIs, Agent Builder), Cloud Run (serverless containers for agents), BigQuery (agent data analysis), Pub/Sub (event-driven agent triggers), and Firestore (agent state management). The AI tier is specifically designed for companies building with Gemini and Vertex AI Agent Builder.
Discount: Up to $350K in GCP credits for AI startups (Scale + AI tier combined)
Eligibility: Start: unfunded startups; Scale: pre-seed to Series A with institutional investor; AI: AI-focused startups meeting Scale criteria
Apply: https://cloud.google.com/startup/benefits

### Google Gemini API (AI Studio)
URL: https://ai.google.dev/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Generous free tier with no credit card required. Access to Gemini 2.5 Pro, Flash, and Flash-Lite at no cost with rate limits.
Limits: Gemini 2.5 Pro: 5 RPM, 250,000 TPM, 100 RPD. Gemini 2.5 Flash: 15 RPM, 250,000 TPM, 250 RPD. Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite: 15 RPM, 250,000 TPM, 1,000 RPD. 1M token context window on all free-tier models. Data may be used for model training on free tier.
Agent Notes: 1M token context window is ideal for long-document RAG, entire codebases as context, and multi-turn agent memory. Grounding with Google Search free up to 500 RPD. Best free tier for large-context agentic tasks.
Discount: Completely free tier, no credit card required
Eligibility: All users in eligible countries (not EU/EEA/UK/Switzerland for commercial use on free tier)
Apply: https://aistudio.google.com/

### Groq API
URL: https://groq.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with no credit card required. Access to all hosted models (Llama 4, Kimi K2, Qwen 3, Whisper, etc.) subject to rate limits.
Limits: Free tier: llama-3.1-8b-instant: 30 RPM, 14,400 RPD, 6,000 TPM, 500,000 TPD. llama-3.3-70b-versatile: 30 RPM, 1,000 RPD, 12,000 TPM, 100,000 TPD. Llama 4 Scout: 30 RPM, 1,000 RPD, 30,000 TPM. No billing required on free tier.
Agent Notes: Groq LPU delivers world-record inference speeds (often 700+ tokens/sec), making it ideal for latency-sensitive agentic loops, real-time streaming agents, and high-throughput pipelines where response time is critical.
Discount: Completely free tier, no credit card required
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://console.groq.com/

### Hugging Face Inference API
URL: https://huggingface.co/inference-api
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free Inference API tier for public models with rate limits. Pro plan ($9/mo) provides faster inference. Inference Endpoints (dedicated) start at pay-as-you-go GPU pricing. Also offers free Spaces for hosting agent UIs and demos.
Limits: Free Inference API: rate-limited (varies by model), shared compute. Pro ($9/mo): higher rate limits, priority. Inference Endpoints: CPU from $0.06/hr, GPU T4 from $0.60/hr. Spaces: free for public demos with CPU basic tier.
Agent Notes: Hugging Face hosts 500K+ models and provides one-click Inference Endpoints. Agents can call specialized models (embedding, classification, image, audio) via a unified API. The Hub is the standard repository for open-source agent components. Datasets, model cards, and Spaces create a complete open ML ecosystem for agent development.
Discount: Free API for public models; $9/mo Pro for higher limits
Eligibility: All accounts; free tier available without credit card
Apply: https://huggingface.co/pricing

### LiteLLM
URL: https://litellm.ai
Type: open-source
Deal: Open-source proxy (MIT license): completely free to self-host. Enterprise Basic: $250/month (Prometheus metrics, guardrails, SSO, audit logs). Enterprise Premium: $30,000/year (priority support, dedicated account management).
Limits: OSS: no software limits, you pay only infrastructure costs. Enterprise Basic: $250/month, self-hosted. Enterprise Premium: $2,500/month equivalent, self-hosted.
Agent Notes: The most popular open-source LLM gateway — provides unified API for 100+ providers, virtual key management, per-key/per-user budgets (essential for multi-agent cost control), rate limiting, and fallback routing. Used by Netflix, Lemonade, and major AI teams. The free OSS version is fully production-capable; the $250/month Enterprise Basic adds guardrails and SSO for compliance-focused deployments.

### Martian Router
URL: https://withmartian.com
Type: startup-credits
Deal: Usage-based pricing billed at model rates — no additional routing fee on top of model costs. Custom Router product available for enterprise (contact sales, billed annually). No free tier listed publicly.
Limits: Usage-based only — pay for model API calls. Custom Router (enterprise): annual contract, contact sales.
Agent Notes: Claims to be the first LLM router and demonstrated outperforming GPT-4 on OpenAI's own benchmarks at lower cost. Sophisticated ML-based routing that dynamically selects the optimal model for each query. The lack of a free tier and opaque pricing make it harder to evaluate than Not Diamond, but the ML approach is reportedly the most sophisticated available.
Apply: https://withmartian.com/pricing

### Mistral AI API (La Plateforme)
URL: https://console.mistral.ai/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free 'Experiment' tier with no credit card required, providing access to all Mistral models including Large, Small, Codestral, and embedding models.
Limits: 2 RPM (global rate limit of 1 req/sec per API key), 500K TPM, 1B tokens/month on the Experiment tier. Standard pool: 50,000 tokens/min, 4,000,000 tokens/month. Mistral Embed: 20,000,000 tokens/min, 60 req/min.
Agent Notes: Codestral is exceptional for code-generation agents. Mistral's open-weight models can be self-hosted for zero marginal cost. Strong European data residency option for compliant agentic pipelines.
Discount: Free Experiment tier, no credit card needed
Eligibility: All developers

### Mistral Embeddings (mistral-embed)
URL: https://docs.mistral.ai/capabilities/embeddings/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier access to mistral-embed as part of Mistral's La Plateforme Experiment tier. 20M tokens/min, 60 req/min, 200B tokens/month on free tier.
Limits: Free Experiment tier: mistral-embed: 20,000,000 tokens/min, 60 req/min, 200,000,000,000 tokens/month (extremely generous). Part of La Plateforme free tier (no credit card required).
Agent Notes: Mistral's embedding model offers best-in-class embedding quality for European-language text with 1,024-dimensional vectors. The massive monthly token allowance on the free tier makes it practical for bulk embedding jobs in agent knowledge base construction.
Discount: Free on Experiment tier with 200B token/month allowance
Eligibility: All users with La Plateforme account
Apply: https://console.mistral.ai/

### Not Diamond
URL: https://www.notdiamond.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier: 10,000 routing recommendations/month, 3 free custom routers, 10 free prompt optimizations/month. No credit card required.
Limits: Free: 10k routing recommendations/month, 3 custom routers, 10 prompt optimizations/month. Paid: $10/10k additional recommendations, $20/additional prompt optimization. Custom enterprise plan for agent optimization and VPC deployments.
Agent Notes: ML-based intelligent routing that predicts which model performs best for each specific query — maximizing accuracy while minimizing cost. Unlike rule-based routing (Portkey), Not Diamond uses benchmark-driven + LLM classifier routing trained on performance data. For agents with diverse subtasks, routing each subtask to the optimal model can reduce costs 30-50% without quality loss.
Apply: https://www.notdiamond.ai/pricing

### OpenAI Agents SDK
URL: https://openai.com/index/new-tools-to-build-agents/
Type: open-source
Deal: Free to use (open-source SDK). Pay only for model token usage and tool usage (Code Interpreter: $0.03/session, Web Search: $10/1K calls).
Limits: SDK free and open-source. Built-in tools: Code Interpreter ($0.03/session), File Search storage ($0.10/GB/day from Nov 2025), Web Search ($10/1K calls). Agent Builder design is free; costs incur at Run. File Search: $0.10/GB/day after free tier.
Agent Notes: OpenAI's official Python SDK for building production agents with built-in tools, handoffs between agents, and guardrails. Replaces the Assistants API with a more Pythonic interface. Integrates natively with OpenAI's hosted tools for code execution and web search without external infrastructure.
Eligibility: All users — open source
Apply: https://github.com/openai/openai-agents-python

### OpenAI API
URL: https://openai.com/api/
Type: startup-credits
Deal: $5 in free credits for new accounts; startup program via VC partners offers up to $2,500 in API credits; Microsoft Founders Hub provides $500 in OpenAI credits
Limits: No recurring free tier for general API use. New accounts get ~$5 one-time credits. File Search storage: first 1 GB free. Startup program credits vary by partner.
Agent Notes: GPT-4o and o-series models are critical for agentic pipelines. Assistants API supports tool calling, code interpreter, and file search natively. Batch API offers 50% discount for non-real-time agent tasks.
Discount: Up to $2,500 in API credits via VC partner programs; $500 via Microsoft Founders Hub
Eligibility: Early-stage startups backed by partner VCs, or companies using Microsoft Founders Hub
Apply: https://openai.com/startups/

### OpenAI Embeddings (text-embedding-3)
URL: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/embeddings
Type: free-tier
Deal: Part of OpenAI API — no dedicated free tier for embeddings. Trial credits ($5) apply. Startup program credits apply. Very low cost: text-embedding-3-small at $0.02/M tokens.
Limits: No dedicated embedding free tier. Trial credits: ~$5 on new account. Pricing: text-embedding-3-small: $0.02/M tokens; text-embedding-3-large: $0.13/M tokens.
Agent Notes: text-embedding-3-small offers exceptional cost-efficiency at $0.02/M tokens with matryoshka embedding support — dimensions can be reduced post-generation without re-embedding, reducing vector DB storage costs for large agent knowledge bases.
Discount: $5 trial credits applicable to embeddings
Eligibility: New OpenAI accounts
Apply: https://platform.openai.com/

### OpenRouter
URL: https://openrouter.ai/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free model access via models with ':free' suffix (e.g., deepseek-r1:free, llama-3.3-70b:free). 20 free requests/minute with no payment. 50 free model requests/day with no credits purchased; 1,000/day after purchasing ≥$10 in credits.
Limits: Free tier: 20 RPM, 50 :free model requests/day (no credits purchased) or 1,000/day (≥$10 purchased). No credit card needed for free models.
Agent Notes: OpenRouter provides a unified API for 300+ models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, DeepSeek, and others. Critical for building model-agnostic agents with automatic fallback between providers. Free models include capable options like Llama 3.3 70B, DeepSeek R1, and Qwen.
Discount: Free :free model tier, no credit card required
Eligibility: All developers

### Perplexity Sonar API
URL: https://docs.perplexity.ai/
Type: discount
Deal: Pro subscribers receive $5/month in API credits. No permanent free tier for the API. Pay-as-you-go pricing. $5/1,000 search requests for raw search API.
Limits: No free tier for API. Pro plan ($20/month) includes $5/month in API credits. Sonar model: $1/$1 per million tokens (input/output). Sonar Pro: $3/$15 per million tokens. Search context fees: $5-$12 per 1,000 requests depending on depth.
Agent Notes: Sonar API is uniquely designed for agents requiring real-time web search with AI synthesis. It combines search + LLM in a single API call, ideal for agents needing up-to-date information without managing a separate search pipeline. Sonar Deep Research enables multi-step research automation.
Discount: $5/month in API credits with Pro subscription ($20/month)
Eligibility: Perplexity Pro subscribers

### Replicate
URL: https://replicate.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Pay-per-prediction with no minimum spend. New users get a limited free trial. Runs scale to zero when idle—no charges for downtime.
Limits: No persistent free tier; pay-as-you-go only. CPU: $0.0001/sec; T4 GPU: $0.000225/sec; A100 80GB: $0.0014/sec. Free predictions for new users on signup.
Agent Notes: Provides a one-line API to run thousands of open-source ML models (image gen, LLMs, audio, vision). Agents can call models like Llama, SDXL, Whisper via REST without managing any GPU infra. Cog framework lets agents deploy custom fine-tuned models in minutes.
Discount: Limited free predictions for new signups
Eligibility: New accounts only

### Together AI
URL: https://www.together.ai/
Type: startup-credits
Deal: Startup Accelerator program offers up to $50K in free platform credits. No recurring free tier; $5 minimum credit purchase required to start.
Limits: No free tier for general use. Startup Accelerator: Build tier (up to $5M raised) = $15K credits + 3 hours engineering support; Grow tier ($5M-$10M raised) = $30K credits; Scale tier (>$10M raised) = $50K credits.
Agent Notes: Together AI supports the broadest catalog of open-source models (Llama 4, DeepSeek R1, Qwen, Mistral, etc.) with serverless inference, fine-tuning, and dedicated endpoints. Ideal for building model-agnostic agent frameworks.
Discount: Up to $50K in free platform credits via Startup Accelerator
Eligibility: Startups at any stage of funding; three tiers based on amount raised
Apply: https://www.together.ai/startup-accelerator

### xAI Grok API
URL: https://console.x.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with $25/month in free API credits. Access to Grok-2 and Grok-2 Mini models.
Limits: $25/month free credits. Rate limits apply.
Agent Notes: Generous free monthly credits make this great for agent prototyping. Grok models have strong reasoning and are less restrictive on certain content types.
Discount: $25/month free credits
Eligibility: All developers

### Sambanova Cloud
URL: https://cloud.sambanova.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free inference API with extremely fast speeds. Access to Llama, DeepSeek, and Qwen models with world-record token throughput.
Limits: Rate limited on free tier. No credit card required.
Agent Notes: Fastest open-model inference available. Great for latency-sensitive agent loops that need quick LLM responses.
Discount: Free inference tier
Eligibility: All developers

### Lepton AI
URL: https://www.lepton.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with $10 in credits. Run open-source models with serverless GPU inference.
Limits: $10 free credits. Pay-as-you-go after.
Agent Notes: Serverless GPU inference makes it easy for agents to call open-source models without managing infrastructure.
Discount: $10 free credits
Eligibility: All developers

### Anyscale Endpoints
URL: https://www.anyscale.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier for open-source model inference. Access Llama, Mistral, and other open models.
Limits: Limited free tier requests.
Agent Notes: Built on Ray, great for agents that need to scale from prototype to production with open-source models.
Discount: Free tier available
Eligibility: All developers


## 🤖 Agent Frameworks & Orchestration
Libraries and platforms for building, orchestrating, and deploying autonomous AI agents. From low-level SDKs to full no-code agent builders.

### AutoGen / Microsoft Agent Framework
URL: https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/
Type: open-source
Deal: Completely free and open-source (MIT). Microsoft Agent Framework (launched October 2025) combines AutoGen + Semantic Kernel.
Limits: Free, open-source. Microsoft Agent Framework (launched Oct 2025) is an open SDK/runtime combining AutoGen's agent abstractions with Semantic Kernel's enterprise features. Supports .NET and Python. Session-based state management, type-safe agent communication.
Agent Notes: Microsoft's open-source multi-agent conversation framework. AutoGen v0.4 was a complete redesign for improved scalability and robustness. Microsoft Agent Framework unifies AutoGen + Semantic Kernel into a single SDK for enterprise agent development. Native Azure AI Foundry integration.
Eligibility: All users — open source (MIT)
Apply: https://github.com/microsoft/autogen

### CrewAI
URL: https://crewai.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Open-source framework free with unlimited self-hosted executions. Free cloud tier with 50 executions/month.
Limits: Open Source: unlimited on own infra; Free cloud: 50 executions/month, 1 crew, 1 seat; Basic ($99/month): 100 executions, 2 crews, 5 seats; Standard ($6,000/year): 1,000 executions, 5 crews; Pro ($12,000/year): 2,000 executions, 10 crews; Enterprise ($60,000/year): 10,000 executions
Agent Notes: Multi-agent role-based framework where agents are assigned specific roles and collaborate as 'crews'. Visual drag-and-drop Studio for non-developers. Strong for enterprise multi-step automation. Open-source core is production-ready and free — cloud plans add dashboards, hosting, and SLAs.
Eligibility: All users; open source at github.com/joaomdmoura/crewAI
Apply: https://crewai.com/pricing

### LangGraph
URL: https://www.langchain.com/langgraph
Type: free-tier
Deal: Open-source MIT library is free. Developer platform plan: 100K node executions/month free.
Limits: Open-source: unlimited, self-managed; Developer (free): 100K nodes/month, self-hosted server; Plus (usage-based): $0.001/node + standby time ($0.0036/min production), requires LangSmith Plus at $39/user/month; Enterprise: custom. Reached 1.0 stable release October 2025.
Agent Notes: Industry-standard stateful agent orchestration framework. Graph-based agent definition with persistent state, memory, and streaming. 1.0 stable release in October 2025 added MCP support, node-level caching, and deferred execution. Powers most production multi-agent systems in 2025-2026.
Eligibility: All users; open source MIT license

### LangSmith
URL: https://www.langchain.com/langsmith
Type: free-tier
Deal: Developer plan free forever: 1 seat, 5,000 base traces/month (14-day retention). Startup Plan available for VC-backed early-stage companies with discounted rates and generous free trace allotments for up to 1 year.
Limits: Free: 1 seat, 5k traces/month, 14-day retention. Pay-as-you-go after: $0.50/1k base traces, $5/1k extended traces (400-day). Plus plan: $39/seat/month, 10k traces/month included.
Agent Notes: Purpose-built for LangChain and LangGraph agent tracing. Visualizes agent execution graphs, annotation queues for human feedback on agent outputs, and Agent Builder for no-code agent deployment (50 runs/month free). Native integration with LangGraph makes it the default observability stack for LangChain-based agents.
Discount: Startup Plan: discounted rates + generous free trace allotments for up to 1 year. Apply at langchain.com/pricing.
Eligibility: VC-backed early-stage companies building agentic applications
Apply: https://www.langchain.com/pricing

### LangSmith (LangChain)
URL: https://smith.langchain.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Developer plan is free with 1 seat, 5,000 traces/month, and 14-day retention. Startup Plan available for VC-backed startups with discounted rates and generous free trace allotments.
Limits: Free Developer: 1 seat, 5K traces/mo, 14-day retention, 1 Agent Builder agent, 50 Agent Builder runs/mo. Plus: $39/seat/mo with 10K traces + $0.50/1K additional. Startup Plan: contact for terms.
Agent Notes: LangSmith is the standard observability and debugging platform for LangChain/LangGraph-based agents. Traces every step of agent execution (LLM calls, tool invocations, retrieval) for debugging and evaluation. Dataset management, prompt playground, and human feedback annotation all support iterative agent improvement. Essential for production agent monitoring.
Discount: Startup Plan with discounted rates for VC-backed companies; contact LangChain sales
Eligibility: Startup Plan: VC-backed startups building agentic applications
Apply: https://www.langchain.com/pricing

### Lindy.ai
URL: https://www.lindy.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan available with limited usage. Pro plans from $25-99/month credit-based.
Limits: Free: limited usage, basic templates; Pro ($25-99/month): more credits, advanced integrations, team access. Credit-based model. AI workflow agent for customer support, sales, email automation, meeting scheduling. Connects to email, Slack, Salesforce, HubSpot, etc.
Agent Notes: No-code AI agent platform for business workflows — customer support triage, email drafting, lead qualification, meeting scheduling. Vertical agent approach pre-packaging common business automation patterns. Competes with Zapier AI but with more autonomous decision-making capabilities.
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://www.lindy.ai/pricing

### PydanticAI
URL: https://ai.pydantic.dev
Type: open-source
Deal: Completely free and open-source (MIT). Created by the Pydantic team.
Limits: Free, open-source. No hosted platform. Python agent framework with type-safe tool definitions using Pydantic schemas. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Groq, Mistral, Ollama. Model-agnostic with dependency injection. Lightweight compared to LangGraph.
Agent Notes: Type-safe Python agent framework from the Pydantic team. Uses Pydantic for validated structured outputs and tool schemas, eliminating a major source of agent errors. Dependency injection pattern makes agents testable and maintainable. Rapidly gaining popularity as a lighter-weight alternative to LangGraph.
Eligibility: All users — open source (MIT)
Apply: https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-ai

### Vellum AI
URL: https://www.vellum.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan: build custom agents, workflow runtime included at no charge (no usage-based fees for running workflows). Credits reset monthly. Pro and Business allow purchasing extra credits.
Limits: Free: limited agent-building credits/month (resets monthly). Pro: $25/month, more credits + purchase add-ons. Business: $50/month. Enterprise: custom. Workflow runtime is free on all plans.
Agent Notes: Comprehensive LLM application builder with workflow orchestration, prompt versioning, evaluation, and deployment in one platform. The free runtime (no usage fees for running agents) is highly differentiated — most competitors charge per run. Strong UI for non-technical collaborators to review agent outputs alongside engineers. Good for teams building production agents that need versioning, testing, and deployment workflows.
Apply: https://www.vellum.ai/pricing

### Dify
URL: https://dify.ai
Type: open-source
Deal: Free cloud tier with 200 message credits. Open-source LLM app platform for building agents, RAG pipelines, and workflows.
Limits: 200 message credits on cloud. Unlimited on self-hosted.
Agent Notes: Visual agent builder with built-in RAG, tool calling, and workflow orchestration. Self-host for unlimited free usage.
Discount: Free open-source platform
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://github.com/langgenius/dify

### Flowise
URL: https://flowiseai.com
Type: open-source
Deal: Free open-source drag-and-drop LLM flow builder. Build agents, chatbots, and RAG apps visually.
Limits: Unlimited on self-hosted.
Agent Notes: Low-code agent builder with visual flow editor. Great for rapid prototyping of agent workflows without writing code.
Discount: Free and open-source
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://github.com/FlowiseAI/Flowise

### Haystack
URL: https://haystack.deepset.ai
Type: open-source
Deal: Free open-source framework for building RAG pipelines, agent systems, and search applications.
Limits: Unlimited. Fully open-source.
Agent Notes: Production-ready RAG framework with modular pipeline architecture. Agents can use Haystack for complex document retrieval and QA workflows.
Discount: Free and open-source
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://github.com/deepset-ai/haystack

### Superagent
URL: https://www.superagent.sh
Type: open-source
Deal: Open-source framework for building, deploying, and managing AI agents in production.
Limits: Unlimited on self-hosted.
Agent Notes: Production deployment framework for agents. Handles versioning, monitoring, and scaling of agent deployments.
Discount: Free and open-source
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://github.com/superagent-ai/superagent


## 🔌 MCP Servers & Tool Platforms
Model Context Protocol servers, tool registries, and platforms that give agents access to external capabilities through standardized interfaces.

### ACI.dev
URL: https://www.aci.dev
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan at $0/month with 1 Control Plane; Team plan at $29/seat/month
Limits: Free: 1 Control Plane, 600+ tool integrations via unified MCP; Team ($29/seat/month, $49/seat billed monthly): multiple Control Planes. Unified MCP bundles that simplify tool access to just two functions for context engineering.
Agent Notes: Open-source unified MCP gateway connecting 600+ tools to any agentic IDE or custom AI agent. The 'VibeOps' approach bundles tools into minimal function surfaces to reduce context overhead. Ideal for agents needing broad SaaS access without blowing context windows.
Eligibility: All users; GitHub: https://github.com/aipotheosis-labs/aci
Apply: https://www.aci.dev/pricing

### Composio
URL: https://composio.dev
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 20,000 tool calls per month, no credit card required
Limits: 20K tool calls/month on free plan; 200K/month on $29/month plan; 2M/month on $229/month plan. 250+ platform integrations (GitHub, Slack, Gmail, Notion, Jira, Salesforce, HubSpot, etc.)
Agent Notes: One of the most critical MCP tools for agents — provides a single MCP server that exposes 250+ app integrations as tool calls. Eliminates the need to build and maintain individual API connectors for each SaaS tool. Designed specifically for AI agent tool use with structured function calling.
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://composio.dev/pricing

### Fern
URL: https://buildwithfern.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan available. Generates SDKs, API docs, and MCP servers from OpenAPI/Fern specs.
Limits: Free tier available (contact for specifics). Paid plans from $99/month per SDK. Supports TypeScript, Python, Go, Java, Ruby, C#. WebSocket and gRPC support (unlike Stainless). On-premise deployment option. Alternative to Stainless for SDK and MCP server generation.
Agent Notes: Alternative SDK generator to Stainless with MCP server generation. Broader protocol support including WebSockets and gRPC — useful for real-time agent communication. CLI-driven workflow vs. Stainless's dashboard approach. Relevant as the MCP ecosystem demands more tools that expose APIs as agent-callable servers.
Eligibility: All users

### Mintlify
URL: https://mintlify.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan available; paid plans for teams. Auto-generates MCP server from your documentation for AI tools to query.
Limits: Free plan for individual/public docs. MCP server auto-generated at /mcp path of documentation URL. Supports authenticated MCP for private docs. Works with Claude, Cursor, Goose, ChatGPT as MCP clients.
Agent Notes: Generates an MCP server from documentation sites, enabling AI agents to search and retrieve documentation content directly. Critical for building coding agents that need to stay current with library docs. Eliminates hallucinated API references by giving agents direct doc access.
Eligibility: All documentation sites using Mintlify

### MuleSoft Agent Registry
URL: https://www.salesforce.com/news/stories/mulesoft-agent-fabric-automated-agent-discovery/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Part of Salesforce/MuleSoft ecosystem. Agent Registry is generally available (Jan 2026) for discovering and cataloging AI agents across Salesforce Agentforce, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Microsoft Copilot Studio. Pricing tied to MuleSoft subscription.
Limits: Requires MuleSoft subscription. Agent Scanners for major cloud environments generally available. MCP server registry and URL-based agent registration included. Pricing part of broader MuleSoft licensing.
Agent Notes: The MuleSoft Agent Registry solves enterprise-scale agent discovery by automatically scanning and cataloging agents across heterogeneous cloud environments. Supports A2A protocol and MCP server discovery. Critical infrastructure for organizations deploying many agents across different platforms who need a unified registry and governance layer.
Discount: Included with MuleSoft subscription; contact Salesforce for pricing
Eligibility: MuleSoft customers

### Stainless
URL: https://www.stainless.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan: up to 5 SDKs, 5 generators (each SDK or MCP server counts as one generator), 5 seats, private SDKs. Also generates MCP servers from OpenAPI specs.
Limits: Free: 5 SDKs/generators, up to 25 methods per public SDK; Starter: $250/month per live SDK (15% off for 3+, 35% off for 5+); Business: $800/month per live SDK. Generates TypeScript, Python, Go, Java, Ruby, Kotlin SDKs and MCP servers.
Agent Notes: Generates production-quality SDKs and MCP servers from OpenAPI specs. As AI agents increasingly need to call APIs, having well-typed SDKs reduces errors. The MCP server generation feature makes any API instantly accessible to AI agents without manual MCP code.
Discount: Free for FOSS projects on Starter plan
Eligibility: FOSS projects get Starter plan free; qualifying startups get Business plan free
Apply: https://www.stainless.com/pricing

### Toolhouse
URL: https://toolhouse.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan with unlimited agents and public projects; Pro plan at $10/month
Limits: Free: unlimited agents, public projects only; Pro ($10/month): 1,000 agent runs/month, up to 1,000 credits, web search, file uploads, Zapier/n8n integrations; Business ($500/month): 165,000 credits/month
Agent Notes: Cloud-hosted tool execution layer for AI agents. Agents can call tools without managing infrastructure. MCP support allows LLM apps to connect to 1,000+ integrations via Model Context Protocol. Particularly useful for deploying production agents without building tool infrastructure.
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://toolhouse.ai/pricing

### Zapier MCP Server
URL: https://zapier.com/mcp
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 100 tasks/month. Connect agents to 7,000+ apps via MCP protocol.
Limits: 100 tasks/month on free plan. 5 Zaps.
Agent Notes: Instantly gives agents access to thousands of SaaS integrations. The MCP server means any MCP-compatible agent can use Zapier actions.
Discount: Free plan with 100 tasks/month
Eligibility: All users

### Arcade AI
URL: https://www.arcade-ai.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier for agent tool use. Provides auth-managed tool calling for agents to interact with SaaS apps.
Limits: Free tier available with usage limits.
Agent Notes: Solves the hard problem of authenticated tool use — agents can call tools that require user auth (Gmail, Slack, etc.) with proper OAuth handling.
Discount: Free tier available
Eligibility: All developers

### Nango
URL: https://www.nango.dev
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 3 integrations. Unified API for 250+ third-party APIs with built-in auth and sync.
Limits: 3 integrations, 3 connections on free plan.
Agent Notes: Handles the auth complexity of connecting agents to third-party APIs. Pre-built integrations for popular SaaS tools.
Discount: Free plan with 3 integrations
Eligibility: All developers


## 🌐 Browser Automation & Computer Use
Tools that let agents browse the web, interact with UIs, fill forms, and perform computer-use tasks in real browser environments.

### AgentQL
URL: https://www.agentql.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free Starter plan with 50 API calls/month, 10 hours of remote browser time included
Limits: Free trial: 300 API calls + 1 hour remote browser; Starter (free): 50 API calls/month, 5 concurrent browsers, 10 remote browser hours; Professional ($99/month): 10,000 API calls, 500 browser hours, 100 concurrent sessions. $0.02/extra API call, $0.12/hr browser
Agent Notes: AI-powered web automation using natural language queries to identify elements instead of CSS selectors or XPaths. Particularly valuable for agents doing data extraction since selectors break when sites update but natural language queries adapt. Built-in bot detection avoidance.
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://www.agentql.com/pricing

### Browserbase
URL: https://www.browserbase.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan with 1 browser hour included, 1 concurrent browser, 1,000 Fetch calls, 7-day data retention. No credit card required.
Limits: Free: 1 browser hour, 1 concurrent browser, 15 min max session, 1K Fetch calls, 7-day retention; Developer ($20/month): 100 hours, 25 concurrent browsers, 6h sessions, proxies; Startup ($99/month): 500 hours, 100 concurrent, captcha solving, stealth mode
Agent Notes: Cloud browser infrastructure purpose-built for AI agents. Provides headless Chrome at scale with built-in stealth mode, CAPTCHA solving, session recording, and Playwright/Puppeteer/Selenium compatibility. The foundation layer for web-browsing agents — agents connect once and get managed browser fleets.
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://www.browserbase.com/pricing

### Playwright MCP
URL: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp
Type: open-source
Deal: Completely free and open source from Microsoft. Published as npm package @playwright/mcp.
Limits: Free, open-source, no limits except your own compute. Provides full browser automation via MCP: click, type, navigate, screenshot, accessibility tree access. Works with any MCP client (Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot Agent Mode).
Agent Notes: Official Microsoft MCP server that wraps Playwright, enabling any MCP-compatible AI to control browsers directly. Integrated into GitHub Copilot's Coding Agent. Essential for coding agents that need to test web apps, fill forms, or browse the web. Released March 2025, rapidly adopted.
Eligibility: All users — open source (MIT)

### Skyvern
URL: https://www.skyvern.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan with 1,000 credits. Open-source self-hosted option also available. No credit card required.
Limits: Free: 1,000 credits, core AI browser automation, CAPTCHA solving, basic support; Hobby ($29/month): 5,000 credits, webhooks, priority support; Pro ($149/month): 25,000 credits, team workspaces, 2FA credential management; Enterprise: unlimited, self-hosted, HIPAA/SOC2
Agent Notes: AI agent for browser automation using computer vision — works on websites never seen before without pre-written scripts. Self-healing workflows adapt when sites change. Native 2FA, CAPTCHA, and proxy support. Usage-based at ~$0.10/step makes it cost-predictable for production agents.
Eligibility: All users; open source at github.com/Skyvern-AI/skyvern
Apply: https://www.skyvern.com/pricing

### Stagehand
URL: https://stagehand.dev
Type: open-source
Deal: Open-source (MIT license), free to use. Built by Browserbase team. Costs only model API fees for AI actions.
Limits: Free and open-source. You pay only for LLM API calls (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini) per action. Stagehand v3 is 44% faster than v2, supports Playwright, Puppeteer, Bun. Half a million weekly downloads.
Agent Notes: The leading AI browser automation framework that extends Playwright with natural language methods (act, extract, observe, agent). Enables agents to perform web actions via English descriptions rather than XPath selectors. v3 launched October 2025 with CDP-level speed and full iframe/shadow DOM support.
Eligibility: All users — open source
Apply: https://github.com/browserbase/stagehand

### Steel
URL: https://steel.dev
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free Hobby plan with $10 in credits/month (100 browser hours). Open-source with self-hosted option.
Limits: Hobby: Free, $10 credits/month = 100 browser hours, 500 requests/day, 1 RPS, 5 concurrent sessions, 15-min max session, 24h data retention; Starter ($29/month): 290 hours, captcha solves, proxy bandwidth; Developer ($99/month): 1,238 hours
Agent Notes: Open-source browser API built specifically for AI agents vs Chrome which is built for humans. RESTful API for browser control from agent code. Handles browser lifecycle, anti-bot detection, and session persistence. Self-host option gives full control for privacy-sensitive applications.
Discount: 20% off on annual plans
Eligibility: All users for annual discount

### Apify
URL: https://apify.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with $5/month platform credits. Access 2,000+ pre-built web automation actors and headless browsers.
Limits: $5/month free credits. 30-second max actor run on free plan.
Agent Notes: Massive library of pre-built web automation scripts that agents can call via API. Great for web data collection without building custom scrapers.
Discount: $5/month free credits
Eligibility: All users

### ScrapFly
URL: https://scrapfly.io
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 1,000 API calls/month. Anti-bot bypass, JavaScript rendering, and proxy rotation.
Limits: 1,000 API calls/month. 2 concurrent requests.
Agent Notes: Handles anti-bot challenges automatically, so agents can reliably access web content that would otherwise block automated requests.
Discount: 1,000 free API calls/month
Eligibility: All developers


## 📦 Code Execution & Sandboxes
Secure sandboxed environments where agents can write, execute, and test code safely. Essential for code-generating and data-processing agents.

### CodeSandbox SDK
URL: https://codesandbox.io
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free Build plan: 40 hours/month VM credits, 5 members, unlimited sandboxes, CodeSandbox SDK lite (10 concurrent VMs). No credit card required.
Limits: Build (free): 40 VM credit hours/month, 5 members, 10 concurrent VM sandboxes, 1,000 SDK requests/hour, 4 vCPU/8GB RAM VMs; Scale ($170+/month): 160 hours, 250 concurrent VMs, 10,000 requests/hour; Enterprise: custom, up to 64 vCPU/128GB RAM. Education/OSS discounts available.
Agent Notes: Programmatic VM sandbox creation via SDK — agents can spin up isolated environments on demand. 1,000 concurrent VMs supports large-scale parallel agent workloads. Instant previews for web apps make it ideal for coding agents that build and test frontend code.
Discount: Discounts for education, open source, non-profits
Eligibility: Education projects, licensed open-source software, developer community projects, non-profits
Apply: https://codesandbox.io/pricing

### Daytona
URL: https://www.daytona.io
Type: free-tier
Deal: Usage-based pricing with free trial available. Sub-90ms sandbox creation optimized for AI agent workflows.
Limits: Usage-based: $0.000028/CPU-second. Free trial credits available. 90ms environment creation, per-second billing, stateful environments between executions. Python SDK, Git/LSP support. Realigned from dev environments to AI agent runtimes in April 2025.
Agent Notes: Infrastructure specifically redesigned for AI agent runtimes in 2025. 90ms cold starts enable agents to spawn isolated workspaces on-demand. Massive parallelization for concurrent agent workflows. Stateful environments let agents maintain context across multi-step coding tasks.
Eligibility: All users

### E2B
URL: https://e2b.dev
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free Hobby plan with one-time $100 credit included. No subscription fee on free tier.
Limits: Hobby (free): $100 one-time credits, 20 concurrent sandboxes, 1-hour max session, community support; Pro ($150/month): $100 one-time credits, 100-1,100 concurrent sandboxes, 24-hour sessions, custom CPU/RAM. Usage: $0.000028/CPU-second (2 vCPU default)
Agent Notes: Purpose-built secure code execution sandbox for AI agents. Allows agents to run AI-generated code safely in isolated environments. Auto-pause saves costs when idle. Sub-second cold starts critical for agentic loops. Used by many coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) as their execution backend.
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://e2b.dev/pricing

### Modal
URL: https://modal.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Starter plan is free with $30/month in free compute credits. Startup program offers up to $25K in free compute credits for early-stage startups; academic program offers up to $10K for researchers.
Limits: Starter: $0/mo platform fee, $30/mo compute credit, 3 workspace seats, 100 containers, 10 GPU concurrency. Pay-as-you-go beyond that (e.g., T4 GPU ~$0.000225/sec).
Agent Notes: Purpose-built for running Python functions as serverless cloud jobs. Ideal for agent inference, batch processing, and background tasks. Sub-second cold starts and autoscaling make it excellent for spiky agent workloads. Supports sandboxed code execution which agents can use to run untrusted code safely.
Discount: Up to $25K free compute for early-stage startups via Modal Startup Program
Eligibility: Early-stage startups (apply on pricing page); academics/researchers also eligible for up to $10K
Apply: https://modal.com/pricing

### Runloop
URL: https://runloop.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with generous trial credits ($50 on signup). Up to 3 running Devboxes, 5 Blueprints, 10 Snapshots on free tier.
Limits: Free trial: up to 3 running Devboxes, 5 Blueprints, 10 Snapshots, 3 Objects; Enterprise: custom. Usage-based pricing with per-second compute billing. VPC deployment option for enterprises. Devboxes are cloud-hosted dev environments for AI agents.
Agent Notes: Enterprise-grade code execution devboxes for AI coding agents. Snapshots and blueprints let agents start from known states. VPC deployment supports regulated industries. Positioned as 'agent workstations' for long-running coding tasks where agents need a persistent environment.
Eligibility: All users; startups and enterprises contact sales for volume discounts
Apply: https://runloop.ai/pricing


## 💻 AI Coding Assistants
AI-powered coding tools, IDE extensions, and terminal assistants that help agents and developers write, review, and debug code.

### Aider
URL: https://aider.chat
Type: open-source
Deal: Completely free and open-source CLI tool. You only pay for the LLM API you connect (BYOK).
Limits: Free and open-source (Apache 2.0). No subscription. Pay only LLM API costs. Supports 100+ programming languages, Claude 3.7 Sonnet, DeepSeek R1/V3, GPT-4o, o1/o3-mini, local models. Auto Git commits, automated linting/testing. No per-request overhead — very token-efficient.
Agent Notes: Terminal-based AI pair programmer with tree-sitter codebase mapping that lets agents understand entire repositories. Extremely token-efficient compared to agentic IDE tools — hours of coding for a few dollars. Ideal for CI/CD pipelines and automated code modification workflows.
Eligibility: All users — open source
Apply: https://github.com/Aider-AI/aider

### Cline
URL: https://cline.bot
Type: open-source
Deal: Completely free and open-source VS Code extension. You only pay for LLM API inference — no subscription, no markup.
Limits: Free open-source VS Code extension (5M+ users). Pay only for API inference at cost with BYOK (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Bedrock, OpenRouter, etc.). Open Source Teams: free through Q1 2026, then $20/user/month with first 10 seats always free. Enterprise: custom with SSO, audit logs, VPC.
Agent Notes: Open-source agentic coding assistant with Plan/Act modes, MCP marketplace integration, and terminal-first workflows. No vendor lock-in — switch models anytime. MCP marketplace gives agents access to hundreds of external tools directly within the IDE. Industry favorite for cost-conscious developers.
Discount: Teams free through Q1 2026; first 10 seats always free
Eligibility: All users; Teams feature free through Q1 2026
Apply: https://cline.bot/pricing

### Continue
URL: https://www.continue.dev
Type: open-source
Deal: Open-source IDE extension free for individuals (Solo plan). Bring your own API keys with no markup.
Limits: Solo (free): BYOK, create/run AI agents, MCP integrations, public agents; Starter: $3/million tokens pay-as-you-go via Continue credits; Team ($20/seat/month, $10 in credits/seat): private agents, RBAC, GitHub/Gmail SSO; Company: custom SSO, BYOK, SLA
Agent Notes: Open-source AI coding assistant for VS Code and JetBrains. MCP tool integration, Slack/Sentry/Snyk connectors. Quality control for software factories — AI checks on every pull request. Standards enforcement via AI agents at the CI/CD level. Fully open and model-agnostic.
Eligibility: All users; open source at github.com/continuedev/continue
Apply: https://www.continue.dev/pricing

### Cursor
URL: https://cursor.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free Hobby plan with 2-week Pro trial, 2,000 code completions, 50 slow premium model requests
Limits: Hobby (free): 2K completions, 50 slow premium requests, 2-week Pro trial; Pro ($20/month): unlimited completions, 500 fast premium requests, MCPs, cloud agents, frontier model access; Pro+ ($60/month): 3x usage; Ultra ($200/month): 20x usage, priority features; Business: $40/user/month
Agent Notes: Leading AI code editor with agentic capabilities including cloud agents that can autonomously complete multi-file tasks. MCP integration allows agents to call external tools during coding. Native support for spawning sub-agents for parallel workstreams. The de facto standard for AI-first development.
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://cursor.com/pricing

### GitHub Copilot
URL: https://github.com/features/copilot
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan for students, open-source maintainers, and verified teachers. Individual plan at $10/month with 300 premium requests.
Limits: Free: students, OSS maintainers, teachers; Individual ($10/month): 300 premium requests, unlimited completions; Pro+ ($19/month): more requests; Business ($19/user/month); Enterprise ($39/user/month). Coding Agent in GitHub Copilot uses Playwright MCP for web automation.
Agent Notes: Microsoft/GitHub's AI coding assistant, now with Coding Agent mode that autonomously handles GitHub issues end-to-end. Uses Playwright MCP for browser-based testing. Free for verified students and OSS maintainers. Integration with GitHub Actions enables fully autonomous PR workflows.
Discount: Free for verified students, teachers, and OSS maintainers
Eligibility: Students, teachers, OSS maintainers with verified GitHub accounts

### Windsurf
URL: https://windsurf.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free forever for individuals with 25 prompt credits/month. Unlimited access to in-house models.
Limits: Free: 25 prompt credits/month, unlimited in-house model use; Pro ($15/month): 500 credits/month, $10 for 250 extra credits, unlimited completions; Teams ($30/user/month): 500 credits/user; Enterprise (up to 200 users): custom. In-house models cost 0.5 credits/prompt; third-party like Claude charge by token.
Agent Notes: AI IDE (formerly Codeium) with Cascade agentic flow for multi-step autonomous coding. Simplified single-credit system as of late 2025. Strong free tier with in-house models running at flat credit cost. Alternative to Cursor with competitive pricing for teams.
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://windsurf.com/pricing


## 🔍 Search & Web Data APIs
APIs that let agents search the web, retrieve real-time information, and access structured web data for grounding and research.

### Brave Search API
URL: https://api.search.brave.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: $5 in free credits automatically applied every month. Independent search index (not reliant on Google/Bing). Privacy-first with no user tracking.
Limits: Free: $5/month credits (approx. 2,000 queries at $2.50/1K for Search plan). Search: 50 queries/second. Answers: 2 queries/second. Credit card required for anti-fraud verification.
Agent Notes: Brave Search API uses an independent index of 35B+ pages, making it the only major search API not dependent on Bing or Google licenses. Ideal for privacy-sensitive agent applications. The Answers endpoint returns synthesized answers, not just links, useful for agents needing direct fact retrieval.
Discount: $5 free credits/month, automatically applied
Eligibility: All users (credit card required for verification)

### Exa AI
URL: https://exa.ai/
Type: free-tier
Deal: 1,000 free API requests/month. Startup and Education Grant: $1,000 in free credits for qualifying projects.
Limits: Free tier: 1,000 requests/month. Rate limit: 5 requests/second. Startup/Education Grant: $1,000 in credits (one-time) for startups or educational projects.
Agent Notes: Exa uses embedding-based semantic search rather than keyword matching, making it ideal for agent workflows that need conceptually similar results. FindSimilar endpoint discovers content similar to a given URL — unique capability for research agents. Returns full text content, not just snippets.
Discount: $1,000 in free credits for startups or education projects
Eligibility: Startups or education projects (apply on site)

### SerpAPI
URL: https://serpapi.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan with 250 searches/month. Access to Google, Bing, Baidu, Yandex, and 80+ search engines.
Limits: Free: 250 searches/month, 50/hour throughput. Paid plans start at $75/month for 5,000 searches. Includes U.S. Legal Shield and ZeroTrace Mode on all plans.
Agent Notes: SerpAPI provides structured data from 80+ search engines, including Google Scholar, Google Maps, and Google Jobs — valuable for specialized research agents. U.S. Legal Shield provides compliance assurance for production scraping agents.
Discount: 250 free searches/month, no credit card required
Eligibility: All users

### Serper (Google Search API)
URL: https://serper.dev/
Type: free-tier
Deal: 2,500 free Google Search API queries on signup. No credit card required. Pay-as-you-go credits valid for 6 months.
Limits: Free trial: 2,500 queries (one-time), no credit card required. Paid: $50 for 50,000 queries ($1/1K), scaling to $0.30/1K at high volume.
Agent Notes: Serper provides Google Search API results in JSON with sub-2-second latency — fastest among SERP APIs. Supports Google News, Images, Maps, Shopping endpoints. Ideal for agents needing real-time web intelligence with minimal latency overhead.
Discount: 2,500 free queries on signup
Eligibility: All new users

### Tavily Search API
URL: https://tavily.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: 1,000 free API credits/month (Researcher plan). No credit card required.
Limits: Free (Researcher): 1,000 credits/month. Basic search = 1 credit/request. Advanced search = 2 credits/request. Rate limits: 100 RPM (development), 1,000 RPM (production). Research endpoint: 20 RPM.
Agent Notes: Tavily is purpose-built for LLM agents and RAG — returns clean, LLM-optimized search results without HTML parsing. Native integration with LangChain and LlamaIndex. Also offers a Crawl API and Research API for deep multi-page research automation within agent loops.
Discount: 1,000 free credits/month, no credit card required
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://app.tavily.com/home

### Bing Search API
URL: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/bing/apis/bing-web-search-api
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 1,000 calls/month. Access Bing web, image, video, and news search.
Limits: 1,000 calls/month. 3 calls/second.
Agent Notes: Official Bing search API gives agents access to web search, news, images, and entity data. Good alternative to Google for search grounding.
Discount: 1,000 free API calls/month
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/bing/apis

### Google Custom Search API
URL: https://developers.google.com/custom-search/v1/overview
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 100 queries/day. Google search results via API for agent research and grounding.
Limits: 100 queries/day. $5 per 1,000 queries after.
Agent Notes: Official Google search results for agent grounding. Limited free tier but useful for light research tasks.
Discount: 100 free queries/day
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://developers.google.com/custom-search

### You.com Search API
URL: https://api.you.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier for AI-optimized web search. Returns clean, structured search results designed for LLM consumption.
Limits: Free tier with rate limits.
Agent Notes: Search results are specifically formatted for LLM consumption — cleaner and more structured than traditional search APIs.
Discount: Free tier available
Eligibility: All developers


## 📄 Data Extraction & Web Reading
Tools for extracting structured data from websites, PDFs, documents, and unstructured sources. Powers agent knowledge acquisition.

### Crawl4AI
URL: https://crawl4ai.com
Type: open-source
Deal: Completely free and open-source (Apache 2.0 / AGPL). Cloud API in closed beta — applying for early access.
Limits: Open-source library: completely free, unlimited. Cloud API: closed beta, applying for early access now. Claims to be 'drastically more cost-effective than existing solutions'. Python-native, async architecture, LLM-optimized output.
Agent Notes: Open-source LLM-friendly web crawler purpose-built for AI agents and data pipelines. Async architecture enables fast parallel crawling. Outputs clean Markdown optimized for LLM context windows. No forced API keys or paywalls. Gaining rapid traction in agentic stacks as a free alternative to Firecrawl.
Eligibility: All users — open source
Apply: https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai

### Docling
URL: https://github.com/DS4SD/docling
Type: open-source
Deal: Completely free and open-source (MIT license). Created by IBM Research, no hosted API yet.
Limits: Free, open-source (MIT). Self-hosted only — no hosted API. Supports PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, images. GPU-optional: runs on CPU. Converts to Markdown, JSON, structured DoclingDocument. 1-year anniversary in 2025 with rapid adoption.
Agent Notes: IBM's open-source document conversion toolkit that became the internet's favorite document parser in 2025. Preserves layout, tables, figures, and reading order for LLM ingestion. No API costs — runs locally. Ideal for organizations processing sensitive documents that can't send data to external APIs.
Eligibility: All users — open source (MIT)

### Firecrawl
URL: https://www.firecrawl.dev
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan with 500 one-time credits (500 pages). No credit card required.
Limits: Free: 500 one-time credits, 2 concurrent requests; Hobby ($16/month or $190/year): 3,000 credits/month, 5 concurrent; Standard ($83/month or $990/year): 100,000 credits/month, 50 concurrent, 3 seats; Growth ($333/month): 500,000 credits/month. Also has MCP server. 1 credit = 1 page scrape.
Agent Notes: The go-to web data API for AI agents. Converts any URL to clean Markdown/structured JSON optimized for LLMs. Handles JavaScript-heavy sites, dynamic content, and anti-bot measures. Has an MCP server so agents can directly call Firecrawl tools. Open-source core available on GitHub.
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://www.firecrawl.dev/pricing

### Jina Reader
URL: https://jina.ai/reader/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free to use with no API key. Anonymous: 20 RPM. Free API key: 500 RPM. 10M tokens free on signup.
Limits: No API key: 20 RPM; Free API key: 500 RPM; Paid: 500 RPM (same rate limit, higher throughput); Premium: 5,000 RPM. Pricing token-based: buy tokens to top up. Reader API prefix: prepend https://r.jina.ai/ to any URL. Search API (s.jina.ai): 100 RPM free, 10K tokens/request.
Agent Notes: Instantly converts any URL to LLM-friendly Markdown by prepending r.jina.ai/ — zero setup required. Used by agents to fetch and process web content in a single API call. Average 7.9s latency for full page processing. Search API also available for web search-to-markdown conversion.
Discount: 10M free tokens on signup
Eligibility: All new users

### LlamaIndex Cloud (LlamaParse)
URL: https://www.llamaindex.ai/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan with 10,000 credits included. Startup program offers additional free credits, dedicated support, and community spotlights.
Limits: Free plan: 10,000 credits, 1 user, 1 project, 5 indexes, 50 files per index, 2 extraction agents. No data sources or sinks on free tier. SaaS deployment only.
Agent Notes: LlamaIndex is the leading framework for connecting LLMs to data. LlamaParse handles complex document parsing (PDFs, tables, charts) critical for enterprise RAG. LlamaCloud provides managed document pipelines. The framework has native support for 160+ data loaders — foundational for agent knowledge ingestion.
Discount: Free plan with 10K credits; startup program for additional credits
Eligibility: All users; startup program for qualifying startups
Apply: https://cloud.llamaindex.ai/

### LlamaParse
URL: https://www.llamaindex.ai/llamaparse
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan with 10,000 credits. No credit card required. 1,000 credits = $1.25.
Limits: Free: 10K credits, 1 user, 5 indexes, 50 files; Starter: 40K credits, 5 users, 50 indexes; Pro: 400K credits, 10 users, 100 indexes. Parsing costs: 1 credit (no AI), 3 credits (LLM), 10 credits (Agentic), 45 credits (Agentic Plus). v2 launched Dec 2025 with 50% price reduction on top tiers.
Agent Notes: Advanced document parser that outputs LLM-ready structured content from PDFs, Word docs, slides, etc. Agentic parsing mode handles complex layouts, tables, and multimodal content. v2 (December 2025) added fast mode and cut Agentic Plus price 50%. Critical for RAG pipelines using complex enterprise documents.
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://www.llamaindex.ai/pricing

### Marker
URL: https://github.com/VikParuchuri/marker
Type: open-source
Deal: Completely free and open-source (GPL-3.0). Self-hosted document converter.
Limits: Free, open-source (GPL-3.0). Converts PDFs to Markdown/JSON with tables, equations, code blocks. GPU-accelerated (optional). Supports 10+ languages. Runs locally — no API costs. Faster than LlamaParse for bulk processing but no hosted option.
Agent Notes: Fast open-source PDF-to-Markdown converter that outperforms many cloud solutions for bulk document processing. Critical for agents doing RAG over large document corpora. Local execution means no per-page costs and full data privacy. Increasingly used alongside Docling in agentic document pipelines.
Eligibility: All users — open source

### ScrapeGraphAI
URL: https://scrapegraphai.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan with 50 API credits one-time (5 SmartScraper pages). No credit card required.
Limits: Free: 50 one-time credits, 10 req/min; Starter ($17/month or $174/year): 60,000 credits/year, 30 req/min; Growth ($85/month): 480,000 credits/year, basic proxy rotation; Pro ($425/month): 3M credits/year, advanced proxies. SmartScraper: 10 credits/page; AgentScraper: 15+10/step.
Agent Notes: AI-powered web scraper using LLMs to extract structured data via natural language descriptions. AgentScraper can navigate multi-page workflows. LLM-based extraction means no CSS selector maintenance when sites change. Python SDK with open-source library available for self-hosting.
Discount: 15% off on yearly plans
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://scrapegraphai.com/pricing

### Unstructured
URL: https://unstructured.io
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan with 15,000 pages processing credit, no expiration date, full access to all features.
Limits: Free: 15,000 pages (no expiration), all features, no minimums; Pay-as-you-go: $0.03/page flat rate for any file type; Business: custom VPC/dedicated instance. Processes 64+ file types. Connects to S3, Azure Blob, Google Drive, Confluence, etc.
Agent Notes: Enterprise document processing platform for transforming unstructured data (PDFs, Word, HTML, images) into AI-ready inputs. 64+ file type support. Connectors for enterprise data sources. The 15K free pages with no expiration is unusually generous — enough for many production RAG pipelines.
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://unstructured.io/pricing

### Zerox
URL: https://github.com/getomni-ai/zerox
Type: open-source
Deal: Completely free and open-source. You only pay for the vision model API you use (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Azure, Bedrock).
Limits: Free, open-source (MIT). JavaScript and Python packages available. Costs = your LLM API costs only. Supports GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, Claude 3.x, Gemini 1.5/2.0, AWS Bedrock. Concurrent page processing (default 10 at once). Outputs structured Markdown preserving tables, charts, layouts.
Agent Notes: Zero-shot PDF/document OCR using vision models — converts any document to Markdown by rendering pages as images and passing to VLMs. Structured data extraction with JSON schema support. Much simpler than traditional OCR pipelines. Perfect for agents ingesting invoices, forms, and complex PDFs.
Eligibility: All users — open source (MIT)

### Crawlee
URL: https://crawlee.dev
Type: open-source
Deal: Free open-source web data extraction library for Node.js. Handles anti-bot protection, proxies, and browser rendering.
Limits: Unlimited. Fully open-source.
Agent Notes: Built by the Apify team. Agents can use Crawlee for reliable web data extraction with built-in request queuing and anti-bot handling.
Discount: Free and open-source
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://github.com/apify/crawlee

### Trafilatura
URL: https://trafilatura.readthedocs.io
Type: open-source
Deal: Free open-source Python library for web content extraction. Extracts clean text from web pages with metadata.
Limits: Unlimited. Fully open-source.
Agent Notes: Lightweight text extraction for agents that need to read web pages. Strips boilerplate and returns clean article text with metadata.
Discount: Free and open-source
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://github.com/adbar/trafilatura

### Doctr
URL: https://mindee.github.io/doctr/
Type: open-source
Deal: Free open-source OCR library. Deep learning-powered document text recognition for Python and JavaScript.
Limits: Unlimited. Fully open-source.
Agent Notes: Agents that process images of documents (receipts, invoices, handwritten notes) can use Doctr for accurate text extraction locally.
Discount: Free and open-source
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://github.com/mindee/doctr


## 🗄️ Vector Databases & Embeddings
Vector stores for semantic search and retrieval, plus embedding APIs for converting text to vectors. Core infrastructure for RAG-powered agents.

### Chroma
URL: https://www.trychroma.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Open-source (Apache 2.0) for self-hosting. Chroma Cloud Starter plan is free with $5 in included credits; usage-based pricing beyond that.
Limits: Starter (Cloud): $0/month base + $5 free credits + usage. 10 databases, 10 team members, community Slack. Usage pricing: $2.50/GiB written, $0.33/GiB/month storage, $0.0075/TiB queried. Self-hosted: unlimited.
Agent Notes: Chroma is the most popular open-source embedded vector DB for Python agents. Zero-configuration local setup makes it ideal for development and testing of RAG pipelines. Persistent mode and client-server mode support gradual scaling from prototype to production.
Discount: $5 free credits on Chroma Cloud; self-hosted free forever
Eligibility: All users

### Jina Embeddings API
URL: https://jina.ai/embeddings/
Type: free-tier
Deal: 10 million free tokens for new users via auto-generated API key. No credit card required.
Limits: Free (with API key): 10M tokens, 500 RPM, 2,000,000 TPM, 2 concurrent requests. Without API key: 100 RPM, 100,000 TPM (IP-based). Free tokens do not expire.
Agent Notes: Jina v3 embeddings support 8,192-token input, enabling direct embedding of long documents without chunking. Late interaction model supports on-the-fly task adaptation (retrieval, classification, clustering) — useful for agents with diverse memory retrieval patterns. Also offers Reranker API with same free token pool.
Discount: 10M free tokens on API key signup, no credit card required
Eligibility: All new users
Apply: https://jina.ai/

### LanceDB
URL: https://lancedb.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: $100 one-time free credits on signup; first month free on serverless plan. Open-source version free forever.
Limits: $100 free credits on new accounts. First month free on serverless (pay-as-you-go). Open-source (Apache 2.0) for self-hosted: unlimited.
Agent Notes: LanceDB uses the Lance columnar format, enabling vector search alongside traditional analytics (SQL, full-text) in a single database. Zero-copy data access and native pandas/Arrow integration make it ideal for data-science-heavy agent workflows. Embedded mode works in-process with no server required.
Discount: $100 free credits + first month free on serverless
Eligibility: New users

### Milvus / Zilliz Cloud
URL: https://zilliz.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier on Zilliz Cloud with $200 free credits for PoC. Milvus is open-source (Apache 2.0) for self-hosting with no limits.
Limits: Zilliz Cloud Free: $0/month, 5 GB storage, 2.5M vCUs/month, up to 5 collections. Open-source Milvus: unlimited self-hosted. $200 free credits available via cost estimator for PoC.
Agent Notes: Milvus handles billion-scale vectors with built-in GPU acceleration. Ideal for large-scale agent memory systems or enterprise RAG with massive embedding stores. Zilliz Cloud adds managed clustering, auto-scaling, and built-in embedding pipelines.
Discount: $200 free credits for PoC; free tier available
Eligibility: All users for free tier; PoC credits require sign-up
Apply: https://cloud.zilliz.com/

### Nomic Embed
URL: https://www.nomic.ai/
Type: open-source
Deal: Open-source embedding models available for free self-hosting. Nomic Atlas platform has a free Starter tier for data exploration. API access via Nomic Atlas (Starter plan: free).
Limits: Starter plan: free. Open-source models (nomic-embed-text-v2, nomic-embed-vision-v1) available on Hugging Face for self-hosting at no cost. Atlas Starter includes data visualization and basic embedding capabilities.
Agent Notes: Nomic Embed Text v2 is a Mixture-of-Experts model supporting 100+ languages with 305M active parameters — one of the best open-source multilingual embeddings. Can be run locally with no API costs, ideal for offline or on-premise agent deployments.
Discount: Open-source models free to download and self-host; Starter tier free on Atlas
Eligibility: All users (open-source under Apache 2.0)

### Pinecone
URL: https://www.pinecone.io/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free Starter plan with generous limits including vector storage, search, embedding inference, and RAG assistant capabilities.
Limits: Starter (Free): 2 GB storage, 2M write units/month, 1M read units/month, 5 indexes, 100 namespaces/index, 1 project, 2 users. Inference: 5M embedding tokens/month per model. Reranking: 500 requests/month. Assistant: 100 documents, 1.5M input tokens, 200K output tokens/month. AWS us-east-1 only.
Agent Notes: Pinecone is the most widely used managed vector DB for production RAG. Built-in embedding inference eliminates a separate embedding API call. Serverless architecture is cost-efficient for bursty agent workloads. Native support for sparse-dense hybrid search improves agent retrieval accuracy.
Discount: Permanent free Starter plan
Eligibility: All users

### Qdrant
URL: https://qdrant.tech/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free forever cloud tier for testing and prototypes. Full open-source self-hosted version available under Apache 2.0.
Limits: Free cloud tier: 0.5 vCPU, 1 GB RAM, 4 GB disk, single-node cluster. Free cloud inference with selected models. Free forever for testing/prototypes. Self-hosted: unlimited.
Agent Notes: Qdrant is written in Rust for high performance. Advanced filtering with payload indexing enables fine-grained agent memory retrieval. Native support for named vectors and sparse vectors makes it ideal for multi-modal agent RAG and hybrid search workflows.
Discount: Free forever cloud tier; open-source self-hosted is free
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://cloud.qdrant.io/

### Turbopuffer
URL: https://turbopuffer.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: No free tier. Pay-as-you-go pricing starting at $64/month minimum usage on Launch plan. Extremely cost-efficient at scale — up to 10x cheaper than traditional managed vector DBs.
Limits: No free tier. Launch plan: $64/month minimum. Usage-based pricing on storage, queries, and namespaces. No free trial credits mentioned.
Agent Notes: Turbopuffer is designed for very large-scale (100M+ vector) workloads with serverless, object-storage-backed architecture. Best for agents requiring high-throughput, low-cost vector search at scale. Used by companies like Notion, Vercel, and Retool in production.
Discount: No free tier; competitive usage-based pricing
Eligibility: N/A

### Voyage AI (Embeddings)
URL: https://www.voyageai.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: 200 million free tokens per account for flagship embedding models (voyage-4-large, voyage-4, voyage-4-lite, voyage-context-3, voyage-code-3, voyage-multimodal-3.5). No credit card required.
Limits: Free tokens: 200M for flagship models (voyage-4-large/4/4-lite, voyage-context-3, voyage-code-3, voyage-multimodal-3.5, rerank-2.5/2.5-lite). 50M for specialized models (voyage-finance-2, voyage-law-2, voyage-code-2). 200M text + 150B pixels for multimodal models.
Agent Notes: Voyage AI produces best-in-class embeddings for retrieval, outperforming OpenAI text-embedding-3-large on most MTEB benchmarks. Domain-specific models (finance, law, code) provide significant retrieval accuracy improvements for specialized agents. 200M free tokens is sufficient for embedding entire enterprise knowledge bases.
Discount: 200M free tokens for flagship models, no credit card required
Eligibility: All new users

### Weaviate
URL: https://weaviate.io/
Type: free-tier
Deal: 14-day free sandbox cluster on managed cloud (Weaviate Cloud). Self-hosted open-source version is free forever with no limits.
Limits: Sandbox: 14-day trial, shared cloud, full core DB toolkit (hybrid search, multi-tenancy, RBAC). Query Agent: max 250 requests/month on sandbox. Self-hosted: unlimited. Open-source (Apache 2.0).
Agent Notes: Weaviate's native GraphQL API and hybrid search (BM25 + vector) are optimized for agent knowledge retrieval. Multi-tenancy makes it ideal for multi-agent environments. Built-in module ecosystem supports direct integration with OpenAI, Cohere, and Hugging Face embeddings.
Discount: 14-day free sandbox; open-source self-hosted is free forever
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://console.weaviate.cloud/

### Upstash Vector
URL: https://upstash.com/docs/vector/overall/getstarted
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 10,000 vectors, 10,000 daily queries. Serverless vector database with pay-per-request pricing.
Limits: 10,000 vectors, 10,000 queries/day. 1 index.
Agent Notes: Serverless and pay-per-request means zero cost at low volumes. Perfect for agent prototypes that need vector search without managing infrastructure.
Discount: Free tier with 10K vectors
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://upstash.com


## 💾 Agent Memory & RAG
Memory systems, context management, and retrieval-augmented generation platforms that give agents persistent knowledge and long-term recall.

### FalkorDB
URL: https://www.falkordb.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free cloud tier with 100 MB storage on AWS or GCP. Open-source Redis-compatible graph DB for self-hosting.
