Limits: Free cloud tier: 100 MB storage, community support, multi-graph/multi-tenancy, graph access control. No TLS or VPC on free tier. Free instances stopped after 1 day of inactivity, deleted after 7 days.
Agent Notes: FalkorDB is a Redis-compatible graph database optimized for knowledge graph queries at low latency. Designed for GraphRAG and agent memory applications with fast Cypher-based traversal. 10-1000x faster than Neo4j for sparse graph workloads according to benchmarks.
Discount: Free cloud tier (100 MB); open-source self-hosted free
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://www.falkordb.com/try-free/

### Letta (formerly MemGPT)
URL: https://www.letta.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier allows 3 stateful agents with BYOK (Bring Your Own LLM API Keys). Self-hosted open-source version is free with no agent limits.
Limits: Free tier: 3 stateful agents, BYOK LLM keys, chat via Agent Development Environment (ADE), run locally with Letta Code. Open-source (Apache 2.0) self-hosted: unlimited agents.
Agent Notes: Letta implements a virtual memory system inspired by OS paging, enabling agents with effectively infinite context through tiered memory (in-context, recall storage, archival storage). Purpose-built for stateful, long-running agents that need to remember across sessions without RAG latency.
Discount: Free tier + open-source self-hosted free forever
Eligibility: All users

### Mem0
URL: https://mem0.ai/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free Hobby plan with 10,000 memories and 1,000 retrieval API calls/month. Startup program: 3 months free Pro access (worth $1,000) for startups under $5M in funding.
Limits: Hobby (Free): 10,000 memories, unlimited end users, 1,000 retrieval API calls/month, community support. Pro: $333/month (3 months free for qualifying startups).
Agent Notes: Mem0 provides a managed long-term memory layer for AI agents. Automatically extracts, stores, and retrieves relevant memories across user sessions. Drop-in replacement for manual prompt stuffing. Supports both user-level and agent-level memory separation — critical for multi-user agent applications.
Discount: 3 months free Pro access ($1,000 value) for qualifying startups
Eligibility: Startups with less than $5M in funding
Apply: https://mem0.ai/pricing

### Neo4j (AuraDB)
URL: https://neo4j.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: AuraDB Free tier: free forever for learning and prototyping. Neo4j Desktop: free developer license for Enterprise Edition. Neo4j Sandbox: free temporary instances with built-in guides. Neo4j for Startups program available.
Limits: AuraDB Free: limited nodes and relationships (small projects). Neo4j Sandbox: temporary instances (expire after a few days). Graph Data Science Community: max 4 CPU cores. Neo4j Desktop: unlimited for local dev.
Agent Notes: Neo4j's graph data model is powerful for storing knowledge graphs in RAG systems, entity relationships, and agent reasoning traces. Cypher query language enables complex multi-hop reasoning across knowledge bases. GraphRAG use cases benefit from explicit relationship traversal not possible in vector DBs alone.
Discount: AuraDB Free tier + Neo4j for Startups program (credits available)
Eligibility: AuraDB Free: all users. Startup program: early-stage startups
Apply: https://neo4j.com/cloud/platform/aura-graph-database/

### Ragie
URL: https://www.ragie.ai/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free Developer plan with 1,000 document retrievals, 1,000 pages processed, 10 GB video streaming, and 10 hours audio/video processing.
Limits: Developer (Free): 1,000 max retrievals, 1,000 max pages processed, 10 GB streaming, 10 hours audio/video processing. Usage-based pricing after limits.
Agent Notes: Ragie is a developer-first RAG infrastructure platform with support for PDFs, videos, audio, and code files. Automatic connector sync (Google Drive, Notion, Dropbox) enables agents to access up-to-date knowledge bases without manual ingestion pipelines. Metadata filtering enables precise agent-level access control.
Discount: Free Developer plan with meaningful monthly limits
Eligibility: All users

### Vectara
URL: https://vectara.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: 30-day free trial with all features included. End-to-end RAG platform with built-in chunking, embedding, indexing, and retrieval.
Limits: 30-day free trial with full feature access. No specific storage or query limits published during trial. Grounded Generation (HHEM) reduces hallucination via built-in factual consistency scoring.
Agent Notes: Vectara provides a full managed RAG pipeline in a single API — ingest, embed, index, and query. Hallucination Evaluation Metric (HHEM) scores factual consistency of generated responses, critical for high-stakes agent applications. Zero-shot query rewriting improves retrieval without fine-tuning.
Discount: 30-day free trial with all features
Eligibility: All new users

### Zep
URL: https://www.getzep.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 1,000 credits/month. Flex plan includes 20,000 credits/month.
Limits: Free tier: 1,000 credits/month, limits apply. Flex: 20,000 credits/month (then $25 per 20K additional credits). Open-source community edition available for self-hosting.
Agent Notes: Zep provides persistent memory for LLM agents with automatic summarization of conversation history, entity extraction, and temporal fact tracking. Reduces token costs by summarizing old context rather than stuffing entire history. Supports LangChain and LlamaIndex natively.
Discount: Free tier with 1,000 credits/month
Eligibility: All users


## 📊 Agent Monitoring & Observability
Tracing, logging, and analytics platforms purpose-built for monitoring LLM calls, agent execution, cost tracking, and debugging agent behavior.

### AgentOps
URL: https://www.agentops.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Basic plan free forever: 5,000 events/month. Supports 400+ LLMs with cost tracking, replay analytics, and agent-agnostic SDK.
Limits: Free: 5k events/month, basic features. Pro: starts at $40/month, unlimited events, unlimited log retention, session/event export, dedicated support.
Agent Notes: Specifically built for AI agent monitoring — not a general LLM observability tool. Tracks agent sessions, replays agent executions step-by-step for debugging, monitors LLM costs across 400+ models, and integrates with major agent frameworks (CrewAI, AutoGen, LangChain, etc.). Replay analytics is uniquely valuable for debugging non-deterministic agent behavior.

### Arize Phoenix
URL: https://phoenix.arize.com
Type: open-source
Deal: Phoenix OSS: fully open-source (ELv2), self-hosted with no limits on traces, users, or retention. Arize AX Free (cloud): 25k spans/month, 1GB/month, 7-day retention. Startup pricing available on AX Pro.
Limits: OSS self-hosted: unlimited. AX Free cloud: 25k spans/month, 1GB, 7-day retention. AX Pro: $50/month, 50k spans, 10GB, 15-day retention.
Agent Notes: OpenTelemetry-native tracing with strong multi-agent graph visualization and embedding drift detection. Built-in evaluation framework with LLM-as-judge. Particularly strong for RAG pipeline debugging and detecting hallucinations in agent-generated responses. The OSS version is production-grade with no feature gates.
Discount: Startup pricing available on AX Pro — contact sales.
Eligibility: Startups and small teams
Apply: https://phoenix.arize.com/pricing/

### Braintrust
URL: https://www.braintrust.dev
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free forever plan: 1 million trace spans/month, 1GB processed data, 10k evaluation scores, 14-day retention. No credit card required.
Limits: Free: 1M spans/month, 1GB storage, 10k scores, 14-day retention. Pro: $249/month, unlimited spans, 5GB storage (+$3/GB), 50k scores (+$1.50/1k), 30-day retention.
Agent Notes: Particularly strong for dataset-driven evaluation of agents. Supports logging agent traces, running offline evals against stored datasets, and scoring agent outputs. The 1M free spans is among the most generous free tiers. Hybrid self-hosting means customer data stays in your VPC — important for agents handling sensitive data.
Apply: https://www.braintrust.dev/pricing

### Comet Opik
URL: https://www.comet.com/site/products/opik/
Type: open-source
Deal: Open-source (Apache 2.0), self-hosted with no limits. Cloud free tier: 25k spans/month, unlimited team members, 60-day data retention. Academic users get free Pro access.
Limits: Free cloud: 25k spans/month, unlimited seats, 60-day retention. Pro: $39/month, 100k spans + $5/100k additional, 360-day retention. OSS self-hosted: unlimited.
Agent Notes: Full-featured LLM observability and evaluation platform tightly integrated with Comet's ML experiment tracking. The 60-day free retention is best-in-class for cloud free tiers. Supports dataset management, prompt optimization, and human annotation workflows for evaluating agent outputs.
Discount: Free Pro access for academic users. Contact Comet for details.
Eligibility: Academic researchers and students
Apply: https://www.comet.com/site/products/opik/pricing/

### Galileo
URL: https://galileo.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan: 5,000 traces/month, unlimited users, unlimited custom evals. Luna-2 evaluation models run at $0.02/1M tokens (97% cheaper than GPT-4 for evaluation).
Limits: Free: 5k traces/month, unlimited users, unlimited custom evals. Pro: $100/month, 50k traces + usage-based scaling.
Agent Notes: Real-time hallucination detection with sub-200ms blocking capability — unique among observability tools. Luna-2 models are fine-tuned specifically for agent evaluation tasks, achieving 0.88-0.95 accuracy at $0.02/1M tokens. The inline blocking guardrail capability is critical for production agents where hallucinations cause downstream failures.
Apply: https://galileo.ai/pricing

### Helicone
URL: https://www.helicone.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Hobby plan free: 10,000 requests/month, 1GB storage, 1 seat, 7-day retention. Open-source (MIT license) with self-hosting option. Startup discount: 50% off first year for companies under 2 years old with <$5M funding.
Limits: Free: 10k requests/month, 1GB storage, 1 seat, 7-day retention, 10 logs/min ingestion. Pro: $79/month, 10k free + usage-based, 1-month retention, 1,000 logs/min.
Agent Notes: Proxy-based logging requires zero code changes — just swap the base URL. Captures full request/response chains for multi-step agents, supports sessions (grouping agent runs), custom properties for filtering agent traces, and cost tracking per agent run. Caching and automatic fallbacks help reduce agent costs in production.
Discount: 50% off first year for startups under 2 years old with <$5M in funding.
Eligibility: Startups under 2 years old with less than $5M in funding
Apply: https://www.helicone.ai/pricing

### Keywords AI (now Respan)
URL: https://keywordsai.co
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free Pro plan: 100k logs, 1k evaluation scores, 5 datasets, 2 LLM-as-judge evaluators, 5 prompt templates, unlimited seats, 7-day retention. No credit card required.
Limits: Free: 100k logs, 1k scores, 5 datasets, 2 evaluators, 5 prompts, 412 req/min proxy, 7-day retention, 1 workspace. Team: $199/month, unlimited datasets/evaluators/prompts, 30-day retention.
Agent Notes: Full LLM engineering platform combining gateway, observability, evals, and prompt management. The 100k free logs is very generous for agent development. LLM-as-judge evaluators can automatically score agent outputs in production. Unlimited seats on free tier makes it accessible for teams.
Apply: https://keywordsai.co/pricing

### Langfuse
URL: https://langfuse.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Hobby plan free forever (cloud): 50k units/month, 30-day data access, 2 users. Self-hosted open-source (MIT license): unlimited usage, all features, no caps. Startup discount: 50% off for 12 months (bootstrapped or ≤$5M raised). OSS projects: $300 credits/month for 1 year.
Limits: Cloud Hobby: 50k units/month, 30-day retention, 2 users, 1,000 req/min ingestion. Core plan: $29/month, 100k units included, 90-day access. Self-hosted: truly unlimited.
Agent Notes: Comprehensive agent tracing with execution graphs, multi-step agent span visualization, prompt management with versioning, and LLM-as-judge evaluators. OpenTelemetry support means it works with any agent framework. Self-hosting removes all limits — ideal for privacy-sensitive agent workloads.
Discount: 50% off first year for early-stage startups (≤$5M raised, ≤5 years old). Apply at langfuse.com/startups. YC companies get additional deal on Bookface.
Eligibility: Bootstrapped or ≤$5M in total venture funding, incorporated ≤5 years ago, privately held
Apply: https://langfuse.com/startups

### LangWatch
URL: https://langwatch.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Developer plan free: 50,000 logs/month, 2 users, 14-day data access, 3 scenarios/simulations/custom evaluations. Open-source and self-hostable with no usage limits.
Limits: Free: 50k logs/month, 14-day retention, 2 users, limited simulations. Growth: from €59/core-seat/month, 200k events + €0.0005/event, 30-day retention.
Agent Notes: Unique 'agent simulation' feature lets you test agents against synthetic user scenarios before deployment — critical for catching failure modes in production agents. OpenTelemetry-native. Jailbreak and prompt injection detection built-in. Good choice for teams wanting evaluation, monitoring, and safety checks in one platform.
Apply: https://langwatch.ai/pricing

### Lunary
URL: https://lunary.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan: 10,000 events/month, 3 projects, 30-day log retention, 1 seat. Open-source Community Edition self-hostable for free.
Limits: Free: 10k events/month, 30-day retention, 1 seat, 3 projects. Team: $20/user/month, 50k events + $10/50k, 1-year history, up to 10 seats.
Agent Notes: LLM analytics focused on conversation and agent session tracking. Smart views, human review workflows, and topic analysis help identify patterns in agent failures. Good for chat-based agent products needing both technical monitoring and product analytics.
Apply: https://lunary.ai/pricing

### Maxim AI
URL: https://www.getmaxim.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Developer plan free forever: up to 3 seats, 1 workspace, 10,000 logs/month, 3-day data retention, email support.
Limits: Free: 3 seats, 10k logs/month, 3-day retention, 1 workspace. Professional: $29/seat/month, 100k logs, 7-day retention, simulation runs, online evals. Business: $49/seat/month, 500k logs, 30-day retention.
Agent Notes: Comprehensive AI evaluation and observability platform with simulation capabilities for testing agents pre-deployment. Supports multi-agent workflow testing, custom dashboards, PII management, and RBAC. Good for enterprise teams needing structured evaluation workflows beyond just logging.
Apply: https://www.getmaxim.ai/pricing

### Portkey
URL: https://portkey.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Developer plan free forever: 10,000 recorded logs/month, 3-day log retention, 30-day metrics retention. Open-source AI gateway available for self-hosting.
Limits: Free: 10k logs/month, 3-day log retention. Production plan: 100k logs/month + $9/100k additional. Logs only count when requests are recorded; unrecorded requests route freely.
Agent Notes: Acts as the AI gateway layer for agents — provides unified API to 250+ models, automatic fallbacks/retries (critical for agent reliability), semantic caching (reduces repeat costs), load balancing across providers, and prompt versioning. The 10k free logs covers initial agent prototyping well.
Apply: https://portkey.ai/pricing

### Traceloop / OpenLLMetry
URL: https://www.traceloop.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free forever plan: up to 50,000 spans/month, up to 5 seats, 24-hour data retention. OpenLLMetry SDK is Apache 2.0 open-source with no restrictions — connects to 25+ observability platforms.
Limits: Free: 50k spans/month, 5 seats, 24-hour retention. Enterprise: custom pricing, unlimited spans, unlimited seats, custom retention.
Agent Notes: OpenLLMetry is the open standard for LLM observability built on OpenTelemetry — use it to instrument agents and send data to any compatible backend (Langfuse, Datadog, Jaeger, etc.). The Traceloop managed platform adds evaluation dashboards, prompt management, and CI/CD integration. 24-hour retention on free tier limits debugging of longer-running agents.
Apply: https://www.traceloop.com/pricing

### W&B Weave
URL: https://wandb.ai/site/weave
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan for individuals: unlimited Weave seats, 1GB/month data ingestion, 5GB storage. Pro plan: $60/month team, 500 tracked hours + 1.5GB data ingestion. Academic license: free Pro features, unlimited tracked hours, 200GB storage, 25GB/month Weave ingestion.
Limits: Free: 1GB/month ingestion, 5GB storage, unlimited seats. Pro: $60/month, 500 tracked hours, 1.5GB ingestion, 100GB storage.
Agent Notes: Combines LLM agent observability with ML experiment tracking in a unified platform — ideal for teams fine-tuning models to improve agent performance. Weave provides tracing, evaluation, and production monitoring. The free tier's 1GB/month ingestion is limiting for high-volume agents, but the platform depth is unmatched for ML-heavy agent teams.
Discount: Academic license: free Pro features for academic researchers. AWS/GCP marketplace billing available.
Eligibility: Academic institutions — contact W&B
Apply: https://wandb.ai/site/pricing/

### Datadog LLM Observability
URL: https://www.datadoghq.com/product/llm-observability/
Type: free-tier
Deal: 14-day free trial. Full LLM observability with trace visualization, cost tracking, and prompt evaluation.
Limits: 14-day trial. Enterprise pricing after.
Agent Notes: Enterprise-grade LLM monitoring. If you're already using Datadog, adding LLM observability gives agents unified tracing across your full stack.
Discount: 14-day free trial
Eligibility: All companies

### Laminar
URL: https://www.lmnr.ai
Type: open-source
Deal: Open-source LLM observability platform. Free self-hosted with unlimited traces. Cloud free tier available.
Limits: Unlimited on self-hosted. Cloud free tier with usage limits.
Agent Notes: Open-source alternative to LangSmith for LLM tracing. Self-host for unlimited free observability of agent pipelines.
Discount: Free and open-source
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://github.com/lmnr-ai/lmnr


## 🧪 Evaluation & Testing
Frameworks for evaluating agent performance, benchmarking LLM outputs, running automated test suites, and measuring agent reliability.

### DeepEval / Confident AI
URL: https://deepeval.com
Type: open-source
Deal: DeepEval framework: fully open-source (Apache 2.0), free to use locally. Confident AI cloud free forever: 2 user seats, 1 project, unlimited trace spans, 5 test runs/week, 1GB-month trace storage, 1-week data retention.
Limits: Free cloud: 2 seats, 1 project, 5 test runs/week, 1GB-month storage, 1-week retention. Starter: $19.99/user/month, 5k online eval runs. Premium: $79.99/user/month, 10k online eval runs.
Agent Notes: The most widely used open-source LLM evaluation framework (700k+ monthly downloads). 50+ built-in metrics including 6 agent-specific metrics (tool use correctness, agent goal accuracy) and 5 RAG-specific metrics. Pytest-like syntax means evals integrate natively into CI/CD pipelines. Run evals locally for free, use Confident AI cloud for team collaboration.
Apply: https://www.confident-ai.com/pricing

### Inspect AI (UK AISI)
URL: https://inspect.aisi.org.uk
Type: open-source
Deal: Fully open-source Python framework from the UK AI Safety Institute (AISI). Free with no restrictions. MIT-licensed.
Limits: No software limits. You pay only for LLM API calls made during evaluation.
Agent Notes: Government-backed open-source evaluation framework designed specifically for agentic and safety-critical evaluations. Supports multi-agent evaluation, tool-use assessment, cybersecurity benchmarks, and refusal testing. Composable 'tasks' make it easy to build reproducible evals for complex agent behaviors. Backed by UK AISI gives it credibility for safety-focused evaluations.

### Patronus AI
URL: https://www.patronus.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Developer plan free: 2 projects, 5 experiments/project, 2-week data access for logs/traces, unlimited comparisons and datasets, $10 free API credits. Enterprise plan with unlimited everything available via sales.
Limits: Free: 2 projects, 5 experiments/project, 2-week data access, $10 free credits. API pricing: $10/1k small evaluator calls, $20/1k large evaluator calls, $10/1k explanations.
Agent Notes: Patronus trains proprietary LLM judge models (including Lynx 8B) specifically for evaluating LLM outputs — outperforms GPT-3.5 by 24.5% on hallucination detection. Strong for enterprise teams needing evaluation automation with custom fine-tuning. Used by Etsy, Gamma, and other production AI teams. Pay-as-you-go API makes it cost-effective for high-volume agent evaluation.
Apply: https://www.patronus.ai/pricing

### Ragas
URL: https://ragas.io
Type: open-source
Deal: Fully open-source Python framework, free to use with no licensing costs. No hosted platform needed — runs locally against your LLM provider.
Limits: No software limits — you pay only for LLM calls used in evaluation (e.g., GPT-4 API costs for running evaluators).
Agent Notes: Industry-standard metrics for RAG and agent evaluation: faithfulness, answer relevancy, context precision, context recall. Widely cited in academic literature which means metrics are well-understood and comparable across teams. No production monitoring built-in — purely an evaluation library. Ideal for teams that want rigorous offline evaluation without vendor lock-in.
Apply: https://docs.ragas.io

### Promptfoo
URL: https://www.promptfoo.dev
Type: open-source
Deal: Open-source LLM evaluation tool. Compare prompts, models, and RAG pipelines with automated testing.
Limits: Unlimited. Fully open-source CLI tool.
Agent Notes: CLI-based prompt testing means agents can run evaluations programmatically. CI/CD integration for automated prompt quality checks.
Discount: Free and open-source
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://github.com/promptfoo/promptfoo


## 🛡️ Guardrails & Safety
Tools for adding safety layers, content filtering, prompt injection defense, and output validation to agent pipelines.

### Guardrails AI
URL: https://guardrailsai.com
Type: open-source
Deal: Open-source core framework (Apache 2.0): free, self-hosted, unlimited validators. Managed platform: first 250 messages free, then $0.25/generated message (self-service) or $0.08/simulation run (hands-on). Bulk discounts available.
Limits: OSS: unlimited, self-hosted. Managed: 250 messages free, then usage-based. Self-service: 3 app connections, 3 metrics, 100 scenarios/hour. Enterprise: unlimited, on-prem, SOC-2.
Agent Notes: The standard validation layer for LLM output correction. Validators can fix malformed JSON, enforce structured outputs, filter toxicity, detect PII, and ensure factual grounding — all critical for agents interacting with external systems. The open-source framework runs in-process with zero latency overhead. The managed platform adds red-teaming simulations to find where your agent guardrails fail.
Apply: https://guardrailsai.com/pricing

### Lakera Guard
URL: https://www.lakera.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Community plan free: 10,000 API requests/month, prompts up to 8,000 tokens, SaaS-only (EU hosting), community support. Enterprise plan: contact sales for custom pricing with flexible volumes.
Limits: Free Community: 10k API requests/month, 8k token max prompt size, EU SaaS only, community support. Enterprise: flexible volumes, configurable limits, SaaS or self-hosted, SSO/RBAC, SIEM integration.
Agent Notes: Real-time prompt injection and jailbreak detection API — sits as a firewall in front of your agent's LLM calls. The 10k free requests covers early development and testing. Production agents typically need the Enterprise plan for volume and SLA guarantees. Consistent latency is critical when using as an inline guardrail — Lakera has invested specifically in this.
Apply: https://platform.lakera.ai/pricing

### NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails
URL: https://developer.nvidia.com/nemo-guardrails
Type: open-source
Deal: Fully open-source toolkit (Apache 2.0), free on GitHub. Also available as part of NVIDIA AI Enterprise (commercial support with pricing).
Limits: OSS version: no software limits. Enterprise version: contact NVIDIA for pricing.
Agent Notes: NVIDIA-backed framework for adding programmable 'rails' to conversational agents: topical guardrails (keep agents on topic), safety rails (filter harmful content), and security rails (restrict external connections). Up to 1.4x better detection rate with parallel GPU-accelerated guardrails adding only ~0.5s latency. Strong for multi-turn dialog agents and customer-facing chatbots where topic adherence is critical.
Apply: https://github.com/NVIDIA-NeMo/Guardrails

### Prompt Armor
URL: https://www.promptarmor.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier available for developer testing. Enterprise pricing on request. Focused on third-party AI risk management and continuous monitoring of AI vendors.
Limits: Free tier details not publicly disclosed — contact for specifics. Enterprise: custom pricing based on usage.
Agent Notes: Specialized in third-party AI risk management — monitors risk from AI components used in your agent stack (third-party LLM APIs, plugins, tools). Useful for enterprise agents that incorporate external AI services where supply-chain AI risk is a concern. Provides continuous monitoring and novel threat intelligence on emerging AI attack patterns.

### Rebuff
URL: https://github.com/protectai/rebuff
Type: open-source
Deal: Open-source SDK by Protect AI. Free to use. Note: archived as of May 2025 — no longer actively maintained. Use as a reference implementation or consider alternatives like Lakera for production.
Limits: No software limits (open-source). Archived — no new features or security patches.
Agent Notes: Multi-layered prompt injection detection using heuristics, LLM-based filters, and a vector database of known injection patterns. Archived in May 2025, so not recommended for new production deployments. Valuable as a reference architecture for building custom prompt injection defenses. The multi-layer approach (heuristic + LLM + vector similarity) is the right pattern for robust agent security.


## 📝 Prompt Engineering & Management
Platforms for versioning, testing, and managing prompts at scale. Essential for teams iterating on agent behavior.

### Humanloop
URL: https://humanloop.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free trial plan: 2 members, 50 eval runs, 10,000 logs/month. Enterprise plan available with custom pricing, SSO/SAML, RBAC, VPC deployment option. Academic and non-profit pricing available on request.
Limits: Free: 2 members, 50 eval runs, 10k logs/month. Service continues uninterrupted at limits — Humanloop works with users to upgrade rather than hard-blocking. Enterprise: fully custom.
Agent Notes: Full LLM evaluation platform for enterprises: prompt management with versioning, A/B testing, human feedback collection, and online evaluation pipelines. SOC2 Type II and GDPR compliant — important for agents processing regulated data. The 25% performance improvement and 3x faster product velocity claims are backed by enterprise customer case studies. Best for teams needing HITL (human-in-the-loop) evaluation at scale.
Discount: Special pricing for academic institutions and non-profits. Contact Humanloop.
Eligibility: Academic institutions and non-profit organizations
Apply: https://humanloop.com/pricing

### Pezzo AI
URL: https://pezzo.ai
Type: open-source
Deal: Open-source AI platform (MIT license). Free to self-host with all features. Cloud pricing details not prominently listed.
Limits: OSS self-hosted: no limits. Cloud: pricing details contact sales.
Agent Notes: Developer-first open-source platform for prompt management, versioning, monitoring, and deployment. Designed for teams that want full control over their prompt infrastructure without vendor lock-in. Good for organizations with strict data residency requirements or those wanting to customize their prompt management tooling.

### PromptLayer
URL: https://www.promptlayer.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan: 5 users, 2,500 requests/month, 1 workspace, 250 eval cell executions/month, 10MB max dataset, 10 playground runs/day. Pro: $49/month with pay-as-you-go at $0.003/transaction.
Limits: Free: 2.5k requests/month, 5 users, 1 workspace, 250 evals/month, 10MB datasets, 10 playground runs/day. Pro: $49/month + $0.003/txn overage. Team: $500/month, 100k+ requests, 25 users.
Agent Notes: Workbench for prompt engineering with full versioning, A/B testing, and analytics. Tracks prompt performance over time — critical for knowing if a prompt change improved or degraded agent behavior. The evaluation framework integrates directly with prompt versions to create prompt-eval loops. Useful for teams with dedicated prompt engineers iterating on agent system prompts.
Apply: https://www.promptlayer.com/pricing


## 🔐 Auth, Identity & API Keys
Authentication, authorization, and API key management tools that secure agent access to external services and user data.

### Auth0 (Okta)
URL: https://auth0.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan for up to 25,000 monthly active users with no credit card. Explicitly includes 'Secure Agentic AI workflows' in free tier features. Includes 1 custom domain, passwordless auth, and unlimited social connections.
Limits: Free: 25,000 MAU, 1 custom domain, 5 organizations, basic attack protection. Essentials: $35/mo for 500 MAU. M2M tokens are a paid add-on on Professional plan ($240/mo).
Agent Notes: Auth0 explicitly supports agentic AI workflows in its free tier. M2M authentication (OAuth client credentials flow) lets agents authenticate as first-class clients. The Universal Login page can be customized for agent onboarding flows. Extensive audit logging helps trace agent actions.
Discount: Free forever up to 25K MAU
Eligibility: All new Auth0 accounts
Apply: https://auth0.com/pricing

### Clerk
URL: https://clerk.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free (Hobby) plan for up to 50,000 monthly retained users with no credit card required. Includes social logins, passwordless auth, prebuilt UI, custom domains, and session management.
Limits: Free: 50,000 monthly retained users (MRUs), 100 organizations, 3 dashboard seats. Pro: $20/mo + $0.02/additional MRU. Users only counted as retained if they return 24+ hours after signup ('First Day Free' policy).
Agent Notes: Clerk's M2M (machine-to-machine) tokens and JWT templates let agents authenticate against APIs and services without human credentials. The robust session management and RBAC system can enforce per-agent permission scopes. Fast SDK integration for Next.js, React, and backend frameworks agents typically use.
Discount: Fully free up to 50K MRUs; no credit card required
Eligibility: All new accounts
Apply: https://clerk.com/pricing

### Stytch
URL: https://stytch.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier includes 10,000 MAUs AND AI agents, explicitly described as 'agent-ready'. Includes unlimited organizations, 5 SSO/SCIM connections, 1,000 M2M tokens, full RBAC—all for free.
Limits: Free: 10,000 MAU + AI agents, unlimited orgs, 5 SSO/SCIM connections, 1,000 M2M tokens, 10,000 device fingerprints. Pay-as-you-go beyond: $0.10/additional MAU, $125/additional SSO connection.
Agent Notes: Stytch explicitly markets itself as 'agent-ready' with built-in support for authenticating AI agents as M2M clients. The 1,000 free M2M tokens are perfect for agent authentication without human logins. No pricing cliffs—purely linear usage-based billing. Built-in multi-tenancy helps agents serve multiple end-users.
Discount: Fully free up to 10K MAU + AI agents; no hard caps
Eligibility: All new accounts
Apply: https://stytch.com/pricing

### Unkey
URL: https://unkey.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 1,000 API keys and 150,000 valid requests/month. Open-source and self-hostable. Designed specifically for API key management with per-key rate limiting and usage tracking.
Limits: Free: 1K API keys, 150K valid requests/mo, 7-day logs, 30-day audit log retention, unlimited APIs. Pro: $25/mo for 1M keys, 250K valid requests/mo.
Agent Notes: Purpose-built for issuing and verifying API keys at sub-millisecond latency from the edge. Agents can be issued unique API keys with configurable rate limits, usage quotas, and expiration—critical for usage-based billing of agent actions. Open-source allows self-hosting for sensitive agent infrastructure.
Discount: Free forever tier; open-source self-hosting available
Eligibility: All new accounts; self-hosting is always free
Apply: https://unkey.com/pricing

### WorkOS
URL: https://workos.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: AuthKit (user management) is free for up to 1 million monthly active users. No credit card needed until production. SSO and Directory Sync are priced per enterprise connection ($125/ea), not per user.
Limits: AuthKit: 1M MAU free; $2,500/mo per additional 1M. SSO: $125/connection (volume discounts at 16+ connections). Directory Sync: $125/connection. Radar (fraud): 1,000 checks free, then $100/50K checks.
Agent Notes: WorkOS is designed for building enterprise-ready SaaS, which agents increasingly need to be. The 1M MAU free tier for AuthKit is extremely generous for agent-powered products. SSO per-connection pricing aligns better with B2B agent products than per-user pricing. Admin Portal for enterprise customers is included.
Discount: 1M MAU free forever on AuthKit; staging environment always free
Eligibility: All new WorkOS accounts
Apply: https://workos.com/pricing

### Supabase Auth
URL: https://supabase.com/auth
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with unlimited users. Includes email/password, OAuth, magic links, and MFA.
Limits: 50,000 monthly active users on free plan. 4-hour token validity.
Agent Notes: Unlimited free users with full OAuth support. When building agent apps that need user auth, this is hard to beat on free tier generosity.
Discount: Free with 50K MAUs
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://supabase.com

### PropelAuth
URL: https://www.propelauth.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 1,000 monthly active users. B2B auth with organizations, roles, and RBAC built-in.
Limits: 1,000 MAUs. Unlimited orgs on free plan.
Agent Notes: Built specifically for B2B SaaS — if your agent platform serves multiple organizations, this handles multi-tenancy out of the box.
Discount: Free with 1K MAUs
Eligibility: All developers


## 💳 Agent Payments & Billing
Payment processing and billing tools that enable agents to make purchases, handle subscriptions, and manage financial transactions.

### Fetch.ai Agentverse
URL: https://agentverse.ai
Type: free-tier
Deal: Agentverse is free to use for agent discovery, registration, and performance tracking. Built on uAgents open-source framework. Provides decentralized marketplace for AI agent discovery and monetization.
Limits: Free to register and discover agents. Monetization features available. Decentralized architecture means no single platform fee for basic usage.
Agent Notes: Agentverse provides a decentralized registry where agents can be discovered, registered, and monetized. Agents register their capabilities and endpoints; other agents or users can search by capability. Native integration with Skyfire for agent payments. The uAgents Python framework simplifies building discoverable agents.
Discount: Free to use for agent registration and discovery
Eligibility: All developers; open registration

### Nevermined
URL: https://nevermined.io
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with full platform access for limited volume. Purpose-built payments infrastructure for AI agents with agent-to-agent payment rails, real-time metering, and instant settlement.
Limits: Free tier available for experimentation. Transaction-based percentage fees for production use. Enterprise pricing for high-volume. Contact for exact limits—pricing details provided during onboarding.
Agent Notes: Agent-native payment infrastructure designed for the agentic economy. Supports agent-to-agent payments (A2A, MCP), credits-based settlement, ledger-grade metering, and DID-based agent identity. SDK integration takes under 20 minutes. Particularly useful for multi-agent systems where agents need to pay each other for services.
Discount: Free tier for experimentation; no upfront commitment
Eligibility: All developers; free tier available without application

### Polar
URL: https://polar.sh
Type: free-tier
Deal: No monthly fees or setup costs. Acts as Merchant of Record (handles tax/VAT globally). Charges only 4% + 40¢ per transaction—20% cheaper than Lemon Squeezy and other MoR alternatives. Built-in support for usage-based billing (AI token consumption).
Limits: No monthly fees. 4% + 40¢ per successful transaction. Stripe's fees (2.9% + 30¢) are included in Polar's cut. International card surcharge (+1.5%) and subscription surcharge (+0.5%) may apply.
Agent Notes: Polar is particularly well-suited for AI agent products: native usage-based billing via event ingestion, metering for token/API consumption, SaaS subscription tiers, and automated benefit delivery (license keys, GitHub access, Discord roles). The event-to-revenue pipeline lets agents log usage events that automatically trigger billing.
Discount: No monthly fees; only pay per transaction
Eligibility: All developers; no application required

### Skyfire
URL: https://skyfire.xyz
Type: free-tier
Deal: Identity and payments infrastructure specifically for autonomous AI agents. Enables agents to authenticate (Know Your Agent / KYA), create programmable payment tokens, and transact using USDC stablecoins without human intervention.
Limits: Pricing not publicly listed; contact for details. Uses USDC for instant settlement with no chargeback risk for merchants.
Agent Notes: Solves a core problem: agents hitting CAPTCHA, login walls, and billing pages. Skyfire gives agents verified identity tokens and programmable payment tokens so they can sign up for services, pay for APIs, and complete purchases autonomously. Critical infrastructure for fully autonomous agent deployments in production.
Discount: Contact for pricing; designed for agent-native use cases
Eligibility: AI agent builders and platforms

### Stripe Agent Toolkit
URL: https://github.com/stripe/agent-toolkit
Type: open-source
Deal: The SDK itself is free and open-source. Standard Stripe transaction fees apply to any payments processed (2.9% + 30¢ per transaction). No additional cost for the toolkit itself.
Limits: SDK is free. Costs come from: Stripe Payments/Billing (standard transaction fees), Stripe Issuing (virtual card fees), and your LLM provider API calls. No minimum spend.
Agent Notes: The official Stripe toolkit lets AI agents create payment links, manage subscriptions, issue virtual cards for agent spending (via Stripe Issuing), and implement usage-based billing. Integrates natively with LangChain, CrewAI, and Vercel AI SDK. Enables agents to be economic actors—paying for services and charging users autonomously.
Discount: SDK is free and open-source; standard Stripe rates apply
Eligibility: Any Stripe account


## ⚙️ Workflow Automation & Scheduling
Platforms for building automated workflows, scheduling agent tasks, managing job queues, and orchestrating multi-step processes.

### Inngest
URL: https://inngest.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Hobby (free) plan with 50,000 executions/month, no credit card required. Also includes 100K events/mo, 50 realtime connections, 3 users, unlimited branch/staging environments.
Limits: Hobby: 50K executions/mo, 5 concurrent steps, 50 realtime connections, 3 users. Pro: $75/mo with 1M executions + up to 20M add-on. Events: first 1-5M/day free.
Agent Notes: Inngest is specifically designed for building durable multi-step AI agent workflows with built-in retries, timeouts, and observability. The step execution model maps directly to agent tool-calling loops. Realtime connections enable live agent status streaming to UIs. 'For anyone building multi-step AI agents, Inngest provides traceability plus retries for free.'
Discount: Free forever Hobby plan; no credit card required
Eligibility: All new accounts
Apply: https://inngest.com/pricing

### n8n
URL: https://n8n.io
Type: open-source
Deal: Community Edition is free and open-source with self-hosting (unlimited executions, unlimited workflows). Cloud plans start at ~€20/mo (Starter). Business plan for self-hosted is execution-based.
Limits: Self-hosted Community Edition: free, unlimited executions, 50 users max. Cloud Starter: €20/mo with limited executions. Cloud Pro: €50/mo. All plans include unlimited users and workflows.
Agent Notes: n8n's visual workflow builder with 400+ integrations is ideal for orchestrating multi-step agent workflows without writing glue code. AI Agent node supports tool-calling LLMs directly in workflows. Self-hostable so agent data stays on-premises. HTTP Request node lets agents call any API; Code node runs arbitrary JavaScript/Python.
Discount: Community Edition is free forever when self-hosted; Fair Code license
Eligibility: Anyone; self-hosted Community Edition has no restrictions for internal use
Apply: https://n8n.io/pricing/

### Quirrel
URL: https://quirrel.dev
Type: open-source
Deal: Open-source and self-hostable — completely free. Acquired by Netlify in 2022; the open-source project remains available on GitHub. Supports delayed, recurring, CRON, and fanout jobs.
Limits: No limits when self-hosted; requires Redis for persistence
Agent Notes: Lightweight open-source job queue for serverless/JAMStack apps. First-class Next.js and Blitz.js support with end-to-end encryption. Good for teams wanting to self-host their task queue with minimal overhead.
Discount: Free and open source; self-host at no cost
Eligibility: Anyone
Apply: https://github.com/quirrel-dev/quirrel

### Temporal
URL: https://temporal.io
Type: open-source
Deal: Temporal is open-source (MIT license) for self-hosting at no cost. Temporal Cloud starts at $100/mo (Essentials). Startup program offers $6,000 in free Temporal Cloud credits for companies under $30M in funding.
Limits: Self-hosted: free, unlimited. Cloud Essentials: $100/mo or 5% of usage (whichever greater), includes 1M Actions and 1 GB Active Storage. Startup Program: $6K in free credits.
Agent Notes: Temporal provides durable execution for long-running agent workflows—if an agent crashes mid-task, the workflow resumes exactly where it left off. Critical for multi-step agent tasks that may take hours or days. Activity retries, timeouts, and saga patterns make agents resilient. Widely used in production agentic systems.
Discount: $6,000 in free Temporal Cloud credits via Startup Program; $1,000 in credits for new signups
Eligibility: Startup Program: companies with <$30M funding. New signup credits: all new Temporal Cloud accounts
Apply: https://temporal.io/pricing

### Trigger.dev
URL: https://trigger.dev
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan with $5/month in free usage credits. Includes 20 concurrent tasks in production, unlimited tasks total, 5 team members, and 1-day log retention. Fully managed—no infrastructure to manage.
Limits: Free: $5 usage/mo, 20 concurrency (prod), 10 concurrency (staging), 5 team members, 1-day logs. Hobby: $10/mo + $10 usage. Pro: $50/mo + $50 usage. Per-run: $0.000025 invocation + compute time.
Agent Notes: Trigger.dev is purpose-built for running background jobs and long-running agent tasks without timeouts. Unlike serverless functions capped at seconds, Trigger.dev tasks can run for hours. Native SDK for TypeScript agents, built-in retries, real-time logs, and replay functionality. Ideal for agents doing long web scrapes, file processing, or multi-step reasoning.
Discount: $5 free usage credit on the free plan; no credit card required
Eligibility: All new accounts
Apply: https://trigger.dev/pricing

### Upstash QStash
URL: https://upstash.com/qstash
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier: 1,000 messages/day, 10 active schedules, 10 queues, 3-day DLQ retention, 3-day logs, max 7-day delay, 1 MB max message size. Pay-as-you-go: $1/100K messages.
Limits: 1,000 messages/day; 10 schedules; 10 queues; 3-day log retention; 7-day max delay; 1 MB message size
Agent Notes: HTTP-based message queue for serverless — agents push HTTP callbacks to QStash, which handles delivery, retries, and scheduling. Perfect for delayed agent task execution, cron jobs, and fan-out workflows without managing queues infrastructure.
Discount: No known public coupon
Eligibility: Anyone
Apply: https://console.upstash.com

### Upstash QStash Scheduler (Cron)
URL: https://upstash.com/qstash
Type: free-tier
Deal: Included in QStash free tier: 10 active schedules, CRON expression support, HTTP delivery with retries. Part of the same QStash product — no separate pricing.
Limits: 10 active schedules on free tier; full cron expression support
Agent Notes: Serverless cron job scheduler via HTTP. Agents can schedule recurring tasks (hourly summaries, daily reports, periodic data syncs) without managing infrastructure. Combines message queuing + scheduling in one product.
Discount: No known public coupon
Eligibility: Anyone
Apply: https://console.upstash.com

### Windmill
URL: https://windmill.dev
Type: open-source
Deal: Fully open-source (AGPLv3) with unlimited executions when self-hosted. Free tier supports up to 50 users with 3 workspaces and 4 permission groups. Cloud free tier also available.
Limits: Free (self-hosted): unlimited executions, 50 users, 3 workspaces, 4 groups. Cloud Free: limited. Enterprise self-hosted: from $120/mo for advanced features (audit logs, SAML, autoscaling, agent workers).
Agent Notes: Windmill supports 'Agent Workers' (enterprise feature) specifically for distributed agent task execution. Scripts in Python, TypeScript, Go, Bash. Built-in secret management, approval workflows, and Git sync make it enterprise-grade. The script-as-UI feature lets agents expose self-service interfaces to human operators.
Discount: Open-source self-hosting is always free; 1-month free trial on Enterprise
Eligibility: Self-hosting is free for all; Enterprise trial available on request
Apply: https://windmill.dev/pricing

### Zeplo
URL: https://zeplo.io
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free Developer plan: 500 requests/month included, all features, 1 team member, 30-day log retention. Company plan at $39/month for 3 team members. Additional requests at $20/100K.
Limits: 500 requests/month; 1 team member; 30-day log retention
Agent Notes: Simple serverless message queue for API-based workflows. Agents wrap HTTP requests to delay, retry, or schedule API calls. Minimal setup — ideal for adding queue behavior to existing REST endpoints without new infrastructure.
Discount: No known public coupon; discounts available for large volumes (contact hi@zeplo.io)
Eligibility: Anyone

### Pipedream
URL: https://pipedream.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 100 daily invocations. Connect to 2,200+ APIs with code-level control and built-in auth.
Limits: 100 daily invocations. 30-second execution limit.
Agent Notes: Agents can trigger and orchestrate Pipedream workflows via API. Built-in auth handling for 2,200+ services removes integration complexity.
Discount: Free plan with 100 daily invocations
Eligibility: All developers

### Make (Integromat)
URL: https://www.make.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 1,000 operations/month. Visual automation platform with 1,500+ app integrations.
Limits: 1,000 operations/month. 2 active scenarios.
Agent Notes: Visual workflow builder with API access. Agents can trigger complex multi-step workflows across hundreds of SaaS tools.
Discount: Free plan with 1K operations/month
Eligibility: All users


## 🚀 Hosting, Compute & Serverless
Platforms for deploying agents, serverless functions, edge compute, and GPU instances. From hobbyist tiers to production-scale infrastructure.

### BentoML / BentoCloud
URL: https://www.bentoml.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Open-source BentoML framework is fully free. BentoCloud (managed hosting) offers a free trial with full platform access—no upfront commitment. Pay only for compute used.
Limits: Free trial: full access to Bento Inference Platform, deploy open-source LLMs or custom models. Beyond trial: pay-as-you-go compute. Priority H100/H200 access, unlimited seats/deployments on paid tiers.
Agent Notes: BentoML lets agents deploy custom models as standardized APIs with auto-scaling and monitoring. The open-source framework is particularly valuable for agent pipelines needing custom model serving, batching, and adaptive concurrency. BentoCloud adds production infra on top.
Discount: Free trial with full platform access; no credit card for trial
Eligibility: All new BentoCloud accounts
Apply: https://www.bentoml.com/pricing

### Cloudflare Workers
URL: https://workers.cloudflare.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan: 100,000 requests/day with no charge for duration. Workers Paid: $5/mo for 10M requests/mo plus $0.30/additional million. No data transfer (egress) charges ever.
Limits: Free: 100K requests/day, 10ms CPU per invocation, 128MB memory, 100 workers, 5 cron triggers. Paid ($5/mo): 10M requests/mo, 5min CPU/invocation, 500 workers, 250 cron triggers. D1 database and KV storage included.
Agent Notes: Cloudflare Workers run at the edge in 300+ locations globally with sub-millisecond cold starts—ideal for agent API endpoints needing ultra-low latency. Workers AI provides GPU inference at the edge. Durable Objects enable stateful agent coordination. No egress fees means cost-efficient agent data retrieval at scale.
Discount: Free tier includes 100K requests/day; Workers Paid adds AI/GPU access
Eligibility: All accounts; free tier requires no credit card

### Deno Deploy
URL: https://deno.com/deploy
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan: 1M requests/month, 100GB egress, 50 custom domains, 1 GiB KV storage, 15 CPU hours, 5 team members. No credit card required. Globally distributed serverless runtime.
Limits: Free: 1M requests/mo, 100GB bandwidth, 50 custom domains, 15h CPU time, 350 GB-h memory, 1 GiB KV, 450K KV reads/mo. Pro: $20/mo with 5M requests + usage overages.
Agent Notes: Deno Deploy runs TypeScript/JavaScript natively at the edge without bundling step. Built-in Deno KV (distributed key-value store) is perfect for storing agent state and conversation history. The secure sandbox model (no file system or network access by default) is safe for running agent-generated code. Deno Subhosting enables multi-tenant agent deployments.
Discount: Free tier with no credit card; commercial use allowed
Eligibility: All accounts; no restrictions on commercial use for free tier
Apply: https://deno.com/deploy/pricing

### Fly.io
URL: https://fly.io
Type: free-tier
Deal: Pay-as-you-go with no monthly platform fee. 7-day free trial (2 VM hours included) for new accounts. Shared 256MB VMs cost ~$1.94/mo if running continuously. No free tier after trial.
Limits: Trial: 2 VM hours, 10 machines, 20GB storage, 7 days max. After trial: pay-as-you-go. Shared 256MB VM: ~$0.0027/hr. Storage: $0.15/GB/mo. Outbound bandwidth: $0.02/GB (NA/EU). No permanent free tier.
Agent Notes: Fly.io deploys Docker containers close to users in 35+ regions—ideal for latency-sensitive agent APIs. Machines API enables programmatic VM lifecycle management, letting orchestrators spin up/down agent VMs on-demand. Fly Machines (fast-start VMs) can cold-start in ~300ms, suitable for agent scaling. Built-in WireGuard private networking for secure inter-agent communication.
Discount: 7-day free trial with 2 VM hours; referral credits sometimes available via community
Eligibility: All new accounts; trial requires credit card for verification
Apply: https://fly.io/docs/about/pricing/

### Railway
URL: https://railway.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier: $1/month in ongoing usage credits (permanent, not trial). 30-day trial starts with $5 in credits. Hobby plan: $5/mo. Usage-based pricing with per-second billing.
Limits: Free: $1/mo usage credit, 1 vCPU/0.5GB RAM per service, 0.5GB storage. Hobby ($5/mo): $5 usage credit, up to 8GB RAM, 8 vCPU, 5GB storage. Pro ($20/mo): $20 credit, 32GB RAM, 32 vCPU, 250GB storage, team seats.
Agent Notes: Railway provides Heroku-like simplicity with modern DX for deploying agent backends—deploy from GitHub with zero DevOps. Built-in databases (Postgres, Redis, MongoDB) eliminate separate provisioning for agent memory/state. Per-second billing means agents that sleep most of the time stay cheap. Docker support lets any agent architecture deploy without modification.
Discount: $1/mo permanent free credit; 30-day trial with $5 credits for new accounts
Eligibility: All new accounts; no credit card required for free tier
Apply: https://railway.com/pricing

### RunPod
URL: https://www.runpod.io
Type: startup-credits
Deal: No formal free tier, but pay-per-second billing means small experiments cost cents. Startup/researcher program offers up to $25K in free compute credits. GPU instances from $0.17/hr, often 60-80% cheaper than AWS/GCP.
Limits: No free tier. Community cloud: RTX 4090 ~$0.39/hr, A100 80GB ~$1.89/hr, H100 ~$2.99/hr. Serverless endpoints scale to zero. Storage: $0.07/GB/mo network volumes. Zero egress fees.
Agent Notes: Cost-effective GPU cloud for running self-hosted LLM inference, fine-tuning, and batch inference jobs. Serverless endpoints with ~500ms cold starts and true scale-to-zero are perfect for cost-efficient agent backends. Community cloud leverages unused GPUs for lower prices.
Discount: Up to $25K in free compute credits for early-stage startups and ML researchers
Eligibility: Early-stage startups and ML researchers; apply via RunPod startup program form
Apply: https://www.runpod.io/startups

### Val Town
URL: https://www.val.town
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 100K runs/day, unlimited public vals, 5 private vals, 15-minute cron intervals, 1-minute wall clock time per run, MCP integration, and 3-day log retention. Pro: $8.33/mo.
Limits: Free: 100K runs/day, 5 private vals, 15-min cron intervals, 1-min max runtime, 3-day logs, email yourself. Pro ($8.33/mo): 1M runs/day, unlimited private vals, 1-min cron intervals, 10-min runtime, 10-day logs, 10 custom domains.
Agent Notes: Val Town is the fastest way to deploy agent functions—a save is a deploy in ~100ms. Built-in MCP integration means vals can be exposed directly as MCP tools that agents call. Email trigger and HTTP endpoints let agents react to events. The collaborative 'vals are public by default' model enables a growing library of agent utility functions.
Discount: Generous free tier; $8.33/mo Pro with annual billing (2 months free)
Eligibility: All accounts; free tier has no restrictions on commercial use
Apply: https://www.val.town/pricing

### Vercel
URL: https://vercel.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Hobby plan is free forever. Includes 1M Edge Requests/mo, 100 GB data transfer, 4 hours active CPU time, CI/CD, global CDN, and serverless functions. No credit card needed.
Limits: Hobby (free): 1M edge requests/mo, 100GB transfer, 4hr active CPU, unlimited serverless function invocations (60s max duration). Pro: $20/mo with 10x quotas + usage-based overages.
Agent Notes: Vercel's Edge Functions (deployed globally in <1ms) are ideal for agent API routes with low latency. AI SDK (Vercel) provides first-class streaming support for LLM responses. V0 (AI code gen) and AI SDK are built for agent-powered UIs. The platform's instant deploy from Git fits AI agent microservice architectures well.
Discount: Hobby plan is free forever; no credit card required
Eligibility: Personal projects and hobby use; commercial use requires Pro plan
Apply: https://vercel.com/pricing

### Render
URL: https://render.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with static sites, web services, PostgreSQL, and Redis. Auto-deploy from Git.
Limits: 750 hours/month for web services. 256 MB RAM. Free PostgreSQL (90-day limit).
Agent Notes: Easy deployment target for agent backends. Auto-deploy from GitHub means agents that generate code can push and deploy automatically.
Discount: Free tier for web services
Eligibility: All developers

### Koyeb
URL: https://www.koyeb.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 1 nano instance, 512 MB RAM. Deploy Docker containers, web apps, and APIs globally.
Limits: 1 nano instance. 512 MB RAM. 2 GB SSD.
Agent Notes: Simple container deployment with global edge network. Good for deploying agent API backends that need low-latency worldwide access.
Discount: Free nano instance
Eligibility: All developers

### Zeabur
URL: https://zeabur.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with $5/month credits. Deploy any language, framework, or Docker container with one click.
Limits: $5/month free credits. Shared resources.
Agent Notes: One-click deployment for any stack. Agents that need to spin up services can use the Zeabur API for quick deployment.
Discount: $5/month free credits
Eligibility: All developers


## ☁️ Cloud Provider Credits & Startup Programs
Startup credit programs and promotional offers from major cloud providers. Get free compute, storage, and AI services to build and scale agents.

### AWS Activate
URL: https://aws.amazon.com/startups/credits
Type: startup-credits
Deal: Founders tier: $1,000 in credits for self-funded startups (no application process). Portfolio tier: up to $100,000 for startups associated with an AWS Activate Provider (VC, accelerator, incubator). YC companies can receive up to $500K.
Limits: Founders: $1K credits, requires company website, pre-Series B, founded <10 years, new to Activate. Portfolio: up to $100K, requires Org ID from Activate Provider. Credits valid 12 months. Must have paid-tier AWS account.
Agent Notes: AWS credits cover the full range of services agents need: EC2/ECS for compute, Bedrock for managed LLM inference, Lambda for serverless, DynamoDB for agent state, S3 for data storage, and SageMaker for custom model training. Credits stack with free tier allowances. Bedrock access is particularly valuable for agents needing Claude, Llama, or Titan models.
Discount: $1K (Founders) to $100K+ (Portfolio) in AWS credits
Eligibility: Pre-Series B startups founded <10 years; Founders tier for self-funded, Portfolio for VC-backed with provider Org ID

### DigitalOcean Hatch
URL: https://www.digitalocean.com/startups
Type: startup-credits
Deal: Hatch startup program (2025 refresh): up to $100,000 in compute credits for accepted startups. 12-month program with fixed monthly credits. Select startups also invited for GPU credit packages for AI workloads.
Limits: 12-month 'use it or lose it' program. Credit amount varies by startup stage and review. Base program: covers Droplets, Managed Databases, Spaces (object storage). GPU credits: separate invitation required. Access to 600K+ marketplace customers.
Agent Notes: DigitalOcean is well-suited for early agent deployments needing simple VPS or Kubernetes clusters without AWS/GCP complexity. Managed Postgres and Redis simplify agent state/memory infrastructure. GPU Droplets provide H100/A100 access for self-hosted inference. The $100K credit tier announced in January 2025 is a significant increase from prior programs.
Discount: Up to $100K in compute credits via Hatch program (2025); apply at digitalocean.com/startups
Eligibility: Early-stage startups; application required; GPU credits by invitation only

### Microsoft for Startups (Azure)
URL: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/startups
Type: startup-credits
Deal: Self-service: $1,000 immediately (no application), then up to $5,000 total after business verification. Investor-backed path: up to $150,000 for pre-seed to Series A startups with referral from Microsoft investor network.
Limits: Self-service: $1K (90 days) + $4K after verification (180 days). Must be new Azure user with MSA. Investor-backed: up to $150K, requires referral code from VC/accelerator in Microsoft network. Credits expire—no extensions.
Agent Notes: Azure credits cover Azure OpenAI Service (GPT-4o, o1 series for agents), Azure AI Foundry (agent development platform), Azure Container Apps (agent microservices), Cosmos DB (agent state at scale), and Azure Cognitive Search (agent RAG pipelines). Microsoft Copilot Studio credits may also be included for enterprise agent scenarios.
Discount: $1K instantly (no application); up to $5K with verification; up to $150K with investor referral
Eligibility: New Azure users (MSA required); $150K requires VC referral via Microsoft Investor Network

### Oracle Cloud Free Tier
URL: https://www.oracle.com/cloud/free/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Always-free tier with 2 AMD VMs, 4 ARM Ampere A1 cores, 24 GB RAM, 200 GB storage. Plus $300 credits for 30 days.
Limits: 2 AMD VMs (1 GB RAM each), 4 ARM cores (24 GB RAM), 200 GB block storage. Always free.
Agent Notes: Most generous always-free compute tier of any cloud provider. The 4 ARM cores with 24 GB RAM can run self-hosted LLM inference.
Discount: Always-free VMs + $300 trial credits
Eligibility: All users

### Hetzner Cloud
URL: https://www.hetzner.com/cloud
Type: free-tier
Deal: €20 free credits for new accounts. European cloud with GPU instances starting at €0.50/hour.
Limits: €20 free credits. Must be used within first year.
Agent Notes: Best price-performance ratio in European cloud. Affordable GPU instances for self-hosting LLMs and running agent workloads.
Discount: €20 free credits for new accounts
Eligibility: New accounts


## 🏗️ Databases & Backend Services
Modern databases with AI-friendly features, built-in vector search, and generous free tiers that serve as the data backbone for agent applications.

### Neon (Serverless Postgres)
URL: https://neon.tech/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan with 100 projects, 0.5 GB storage/project, 100 CU-hours/month, and 60K MAUs for Auth. Startup program: up to $100,000 in credits for venture-funded startups.
Limits: Free: 100 projects, 100 CU-hours/month per project, 0.5 GB storage, 2 CU compute (8 GB RAM), 10 branches/project, 60K Neon Auth MAUs, 6-hour time travel, unlimited team members.
Agent Notes: Neon's branching feature allows instant database clones for agent testing environments — each test run can branch the DB, run the agent, and discard changes. Serverless auto-scaling means agent workloads with bursty query patterns don't over-provision compute. Supports pgvector for vector search.
Discount: Up to $100,000 in credits for venture-funded startups
Eligibility: All users for free plan; venture-funded startups for startup program
Apply: https://neon.tech/pricing

### PlanetScale
URL: https://planetscale.com/
Type: discount
Deal: No free tier. Base plan starts at $5/month for Postgres single node. MySQL Vitess-based offering has been the historical focus; now expanding to Postgres.
Limits: No free tier. Base plan: $5/month for 10 GB storage. Scaler: $29/month. Unlimited read replicas on all paid plans.
Agent Notes: PlanetScale offers horizontal sharding and unlimited read replicas, making it suitable for agent applications that need globally distributed, low-latency data access. Best for high-read workloads common in production agent deployments serving many users concurrently.
Discount: No free tier; competitive per-GB pricing
Eligibility: N/A

### Supabase
URL: https://supabase.com/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan with 2 active projects, 500 MB database, 50,000 MAU auth, 1 GB storage, and 500K edge function invocations/month. pgvector extension included for vector search.
Limits: Free: 2 active projects (paused after 1 week inactivity), 500 MB DB, shared CPU + 500 MB RAM, unlimited API requests, 50K MAUs, 1 GB storage, 5 GB egress, 200 concurrent Realtime connections, 500K edge function invocations/month. Startup program: $300 in credits.
Agent Notes: Supabase includes pgvector as a first-class citizen, enabling vector similarity search alongside traditional PostgreSQL queries in a single database. Ideal for agents that need both structured data (user profiles, settings) and semantic search (embeddings) in one place. Realtime subscriptions enable event-driven agent triggers.
Discount: $300 in credits via startup program
Eligibility: All users for free plan; startup program for qualifying startups

### Turso (libSQL / SQLite for production)
URL: https://turso.tech/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free plan with 100 databases, 5 GB storage, 500M rows read/month, and 10M rows written/month. No credit card required.
Limits: Free: 100 databases, 100 Monthly Active Databases, 5 GB total storage, 500M rows read/month, 10M rows written/month, 3 GB monthly syncs, 1-day point-in-time restore.
Agent Notes: Turso provides edge-deployed SQLite instances, enabling agents to run data queries with <1ms latency from edge locations worldwide. libSQL fork enables multi-tenant agent architectures where each user gets their own isolated SQLite DB. Embedded replicas run SQLite in-process within agent code.
Discount: Free plan, no credit card required
Eligibility: All users

### Xata
URL: https://xata.io/
Type: free-tier
Deal: $100 credit for 14-day trial on Standard plan. Includes built-in full-text search, vector search, and AI query features powered by PostgreSQL.
Limits: $100 credit trial (14 days) for Standard plan features. No permanent free tier; paid plans start after trial. Vector search, full-text search, and aggregate functions included.
Agent Notes: Xata is a serverless Postgres platform with first-class full-text search and vector search built in — no separate search service needed. TypeScript-first SDK with auto-generated types makes it ideal for TypeScript-based agent frameworks. File attachments with metadata enable multi-modal agent knowledge stores.
Discount: $100 credit for 14-day trial
Eligibility: All new users

### CockroachDB Serverless
URL: https://www.cockroachlabs.com/pricing/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 10 GiB storage and 50M Request Units/month. Distributed SQL database.
Limits: 10 GiB storage, 50M RUs/month. 1 free cluster.
Agent Notes: Distributed SQL means agents can rely on high availability. Auto-scaling and no cold starts make it suitable for production agent workloads.
Discount: Free serverless tier
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://www.cockroachlabs.com

### MongoDB Atlas
URL: https://www.mongodb.com/pricing
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 512 MB storage. Includes Atlas Vector Search for semantic search capabilities.
Limits: 512 MB storage. Shared cluster. 100 max connections.
Agent Notes: Atlas Vector Search adds semantic search to your document database. Agents can store structured data and do vector similarity search in one place.
Discount: MongoDB for Startups: up to $5,000 in credits
Eligibility: Pre-Series A startups
Apply: https://www.mongodb.com/startups

### Redis Cloud
URL: https://redis.io/pricing/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 30 MB database. Includes Redis Stack with vector search, JSON, and time series modules.
Limits: 30 MB storage. 30 connections.
Agent Notes: Redis Stack includes vector search module — agents get ultra-fast caching plus semantic search in one service. Sub-millisecond latency.
Discount: Free 30 MB database
Eligibility: All developers
Apply: https://redis.io


## 📬 Email & Communication APIs
APIs for sending emails, SMS, push notifications, and in-app messages. Let agents communicate with users and external services.

### Amazon SES
URL: https://aws.amazon.com/ses/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier: 3,000 message charges/month for first 12 months (covers both sending and receiving). New AWS accounts (from July 15, 2025) get $200 in general AWS credits instead. After free tier: $0.10/1,000 emails.
Limits: 3,000 message charges/month for 12 months; or $200 AWS credits for new accounts; $0.12/GB for attachments
Agent Notes: Cheapest email at scale ($0.0001/email after free tier). Requires AWS account and some setup complexity. Ideal for high-volume agents sending millions of emails. Plunk.so is an open-source wrapper that simplifies SES.
Discount: $200 AWS credits for new accounts signed up after July 15, 2025
Eligibility: New AWS accounts
Apply: https://portal.aws.amazon.com/billing/signup

### Brevo (formerly Sendinblue)
URL: https://www.brevo.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier: 300 emails/day (unlimited contacts stored). No credit card required. Includes email API, SMTP, marketing automation, and transactional templates.
Limits: 300 emails/day; unlimited stored contacts; Brevo branding on emails
Agent Notes: Solid free email tier (300/day vs Resend's 100/day). Unlimited contacts makes it attractive for agents managing large mailing lists. Also offers SMS, WhatsApp, and push notifications under one API umbrella.
Discount: No known public coupon
Eligibility: Anyone
Apply: https://app.brevo.com/account/register

### Courier
URL: https://www.courier.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier: 10,000 notifications/month. Usage-based pricing scales from there. Supports email, push, SMS, in-app, and chat from a single API.
Limits: 10,000 notifications/month free; Pro plan at $0.005/notification
Agent Notes: Unified notification API with smart routing logic—can auto-fallback across channels if one fails. Good for agents with complex notification routing requirements. Has MCP server + CLI for AI tooling integration.
Discount: No known public coupon
Eligibility: Anyone
Apply: https://app.courier.com/signup

### Knock
URL: https://knock.app
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free Developer tier: 10,000 messages/month, 500 guide active users/month, 500 AI agent credits/month (then $0.01/credit). Unlimited notification workflows, channels, team members.
Limits: 10,000 messages/month; 500 guide active users; 500 AI agent credits; Knock branding on free tier
Agent Notes: The only notification platform with native AI agent credits — purpose-built for agentic workflows. Supports batch/delay notifications, multi-tenancy, and cross-channel delivery. Ideal for orchestrating agent notifications at scale.
Discount: No known public coupon
Eligibility: Anyone
Apply: https://dashboard.knock.app/signup

### Loops
URL: https://loops.so
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier: up to 1,000 stored contacts, 4,000 emails/month. All platform features included, with 'Powered by Loops' footer branding.
Limits: 1,000 subscribers; 4,000 sends/month; Loops branding on emails
Agent Notes: Purpose-built for SaaS transactional + marketing emails. Ideal for AI-product agents that need to send onboarding sequences, weekly digests, and user notifications with a unified API. Strong Next.js integration.
Discount: No known public coupon
Eligibility: Anyone
Apply: https://app.loops.so/register

### MailerSend
URL: https://www.mailersend.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier: 500 emails/month, max 100/day. Includes email API, SMTP relay, webhooks, inbound routing, email tracking, real-time activity.
Limits: 500 emails/month; 100 emails/day max; 1 sending domain; 1 API token; 100 daily API requests; 24-hour activity retention
Agent Notes: Part of the MailerLite family. Good transactional email with a clean API. Free tier is limited (updated Dec 2025 from 3,000 to 500/month); consider the $7/month Hobby plan for 5,000/month at small scale.
Discount: No known public coupon
Eligibility: Anyone
Apply: https://app.mailersend.com/register

### Mailgun
URL: https://www.mailgun.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier: 100 emails/day indefinitely. Includes RESTful API, SMTP relay, tracking, analytics, webhooks, 1 custom domain, 1-day log retention.
Limits: 100 emails/day; 1 custom domain; 2 API keys; 1 inbound route; 1-day log retention; 1 user/account
Agent Notes: Battle-tested transactional email with strong deliverability. Useful for agents needing programmatic email with inbound parsing (agent can receive replies). Foundation plan at $35/month unlocks 10k emails/month.
Discount: No known public coupon
Eligibility: Anyone
Apply: https://signup.mailgun.com/new/signup

### Novu
URL: https://novu.co
Type: open-source
Deal: Free tier: 10,000 notifications/month, no credit card required. Includes all notification channels (email, SMS, push, in-app, chat). Open-source core.
Limits: 10,000 notifications/month; Starter plan at $30/month for 30,000/month
Agent Notes: Open-source notification infrastructure with a unified API for all channels. Perfect for agents that need to send multi-channel notifications (email + Slack + SMS + in-app) via a single abstraction layer. Self-hostable.
Discount: No known public coupon; open-source self-hosting is free
Eligibility: Anyone
Apply: https://dashboard.novu.co/auth/sign-up

### Plunk
URL: https://www.useplunk.com
Type: open-source
Deal: Free tier: 1,000 emails/month with no credit card required. After free tier: $0.001/email (pay-as-you-go). Unlimited contacts on all plans. Fully open-source and self-hostable.
Limits: 1,000 emails/month free; Plunk branding on free plan; unlimited contacts
Agent Notes: Open-source alternative to Resend/Mailgun built on AWS SES. Self-hostable for full control. $0.001/email is 10x cheaper than Mailchimp at scale. Good for agents needing both transactional and marketing email in one platform.
Discount: No known public coupon; self-hosting is free
Eligibility: Anyone

### Postmark
URL: https://postmarkapp.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Developer free tier: 100 emails/month, never expires. Startup credit: $75 account credit for launched products not yet funded.
Limits: 100 emails/month on free tier; no overages allowed
Agent Notes: Best-in-class deliverability for transactional email. Free startup credit of $75 available for early-stage teams. Ideal for agent-generated receipts, confirmations, and critical transactional messages.
Discount: $75 account credit for startups (contact support, must be launched + charging + no outside investment)
Eligibility: Launched product, charging customers, no outside investment
Apply: https://account.postmarkapp.com/sign_up

### Resend
URL: https://resend.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier: 100 emails/day, 1 custom domain, 1 webhook endpoint, 1-day data retention. No credit card required.
Limits: 100 emails/day; 1 custom domain; 1 webhook endpoint; 1-day data retention
Agent Notes: Preferred transactional email API for AI agents built with Next.js/Vercel stacks. Developer-first SDK with React email templates. Best for sending agent output summaries, notifications, and reports via email.
Discount: No known public coupon
Eligibility: Anyone
Apply: https://resend.com/signup

### Twilio
URL: https://www.twilio.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free trial: $15 in pre-loaded trial credits, no credit card required. Covers SMS ($0.0083/msg), voice calls, and other APIs. Trial restricts sending to verified numbers only.
Limits: $15 trial credits; 1 phone number; can only send to verified numbers during trial; SMS starts at $0.0083/msg after trial
Agent Notes: The go-to SMS/voice API for agent alerting, 2FA, and human-in-the-loop notifications. Pay-as-you-go makes it viable at any scale. Agents can trigger SMS alerts on task completion, errors, or approval requests.
Discount: $15 free trial credits for all new accounts
Eligibility: All new accounts
Apply: https://www.twilio.com/try-twilio

### Vonage (Nexmo)
URL: https://www.vonage.com/communications-apis/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free trial with usage credits on sign-up (Video API: 2,000 participant minutes + 500 advanced feature minutes for 1 year). SMS API trial available with test message restrictions. Pay-as-you-go after trial.
Limits: Trial credits vary by product; SMS trial messages include '[FREE SMS DEMO, TEST MESSAGE]' text; cannot buy virtual numbers in trial
Agent Notes: Strong alternative to Twilio for SMS, voice, and video APIs. SMS pricing from $0.00809/msg US. Good for agents needing multi-channel communications (SMS + voice + video) from a single vendor.
Discount: Free trial credits on sign-up (amount varies by API product)
Eligibility: All new accounts
Apply: https://ui.idp.vonage.com/ui/auth/registration

### Mailtrap
URL: https://mailtrap.io
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with 1,000 emails/month for testing. Email sandbox for development plus production sending.
Limits: 1,000 emails/month sending. 100 test emails/month.
Agent Notes: Email sandbox is perfect for testing agent email workflows without sending real emails. Switch to production sending when ready.
Discount: Free tier with 1K emails/month
Eligibility: All developers

### OneSignal
URL: https://onesignal.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier with unlimited push notifications. Supports web push, mobile, email, SMS, and in-app messaging.
Limits: Unlimited push on free plan. 10,000 subscribers for email.
Agent Notes: Agents can send multi-channel notifications to users via a single API. Free unlimited push is rare.
Discount: Free unlimited push notifications
Eligibility: All developers

### Ntfy
URL: https://ntfy.sh
Type: open-source
Deal: Free open-source push notification service. Send notifications to phones via simple HTTP PUT/POST.
Limits: Unlimited on self-hosted. Hosted service has rate limits.
Agent Notes: Dead simple notification sending — agents just POST to a URL. No SDKs needed. Perfect for agent alerting and status updates.
Discount: Free and open-source
Eligibility: All users
Apply: https://github.com/binwiederhier/ntfy


## 📁 Storage, Caching & Queues
Object storage, caching layers, and message queues for agent data persistence, fast retrieval, and async task processing.

### Cloudflare R2
URL: https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/
Type: free-tier
Deal: Permanent free tier: 10 GB storage/month, 1 million Class A operations/month (writes/lists), 10 million Class B operations/month (reads). Zero egress fees — free data transfer to internet.
Limits: 10 GB storage; 1M write operations/month; 10M read operations/month; no egress fees ever
Agent Notes: Best free object storage for AI agents due to zero egress costs. Ideal for storing agent outputs, generated files, audio/video, and large datasets. S3-compatible API means drop-in replacement for existing S3 code.
Discount: No coupon needed; free tier is permanent
Eligibility: Anyone with a Cloudflare account
Apply: https://dash.cloudflare.com/sign-up

### Momento Cache
URL: https://www.gomomento.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: On-demand free tier: first 5 million operations free each month. After that: $1.00/million operations. No provisioning required, scales instantly.
Limits: 5M operations/month free; $1/million operations after; $0.50/GB transferred
Agent Notes: Serverless cache with no cold starts or VPC complexity. Better fit than Redis for purely serverless agent stacks. No infrastructure to manage — ideal when agents need low-latency key-value lookups without managing Redis clusters.
Discount: No known public coupon
Eligibility: Anyone
Apply: https://console.gomomento.com/signin

### Supabase Storage
URL: https://supabase.com/storage
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier (included in Supabase free plan): 1 GB file storage, 5 GB egress, 5 GB cached egress, 50 MB max file upload size, basic CDN.
Limits: 1 GB storage; 5 GB egress; 50 MB max single file; basic CDN only
Agent Notes: Integrated storage within the Supabase ecosystem (Postgres + Auth + Storage + Realtime). Best for agents already using Supabase as their backend — avoids managing a separate storage vendor. S3-compatible API.
Discount: No known public coupon
Eligibility: Anyone
Apply: https://supabase.com/dashboard/sign-up

### Uploadthing
URL: https://uploadthing.com
Type: free-tier
Deal: Free tier: 2 GB app storage, 7-day audit log retention, unlimited uploads and downloads.
Limits: 2 GB storage; 7-day audit log retention
